subject: IGNITE 25 w/Perry Stone, Darryl Strawberry + more Starts TONIGHT!
body:
Tonight at 7pm CT, we will kickoff IGNITE 2025 at Sheridan.Church! I will be preaching a message entitled, "FEED THE FIRE!" Join me and let's experi
| | |
---
Tonight at 7pm CT, we will kickoff **IGNITE 2025** at [Sheridan.Church](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=24345174.12684.1.4d5ec99ec066c4a53eb31dea2445fded)! I will be preaching a message entitled, **"FEED THE FIRE!"** Join me and let's experience the power of God together! ] **IGNITE Service Schedule:** -Wednesday, September 3 @7pm CT: **Jackson Lahmeyer** -Sunday, September 7 @10am + 6pm CT: **Perry Stone** -Sunday, September 14 @10am CT: **Darryl Strawberry** -Sunday, September 21 @10am CT: **Dr. Randy Caldwell** -Sunday, September 28 @10am CT: **Bishop Darrell Hines** September will be an _INCREDIBLE_ month at [Sheridan.Church](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=24345174.12685.1.3b21ff82d22ea7b06b1a92178f10b06c)!
---
---
## Churches Are UNDER ATTACK
---
| [ ](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=24345174.12686.1.c263ef48b110cf4168914952710321d4)
---
Right now there is a **MAJOR escalation** of attacks upon Churches in the United States of America. Since 2018 there has been an **increase of over 700%** according to the **Family Research Council**. In 2018 there were 50 reported attacks on churches (**less than 1 per week**). In 2024 there were 415 reported attacks on churches (**more than 1 per day**). I want to **offer two thoughts**. **First to the Pastors** reading this.
Take measures at your church to prevent these attacks. At [Sheridan.Church](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=24345174.12687.1.9ea4838bb0c1cd0f1c9175011d577128), I am beyond grateful for our security team that watches over the House every service to make sure our people are _SAFE_. But we also partner with local law enforcement every service as well. The police car stationed at the front of our church, I believe, will prevent 99% of bad actors from even attempting something they should not. **We must be as wise as serpents and as harmless as doves.** **Second to Believers** reading this.
Don't you dare stop attending church! God has not given us a spirit of fear but instead one of power, love and a sound mind (**2 Tim 1:7** ). We are told in **Hebrews 10** that as the Day of the Lord gets near that many believers will stop attending church but we are command not to stop gathering together because we need one another. **Do not let the enemy intimidate you from going to the House of the Lord**. Although the stats are _disheartening_ , history proves that whenever the Church is attacked, she always _flourishes_. These attacks will backfire on the enemy and the Church of the Lord Jesus Christ in America will prevail and be even more effective because of them. **Pray for the Body of Christ and the local churches throughout America.** Thank you,
Pastor Jackson Lahmeyer
---
---
## Progress At Sheridan.Church
---
| [ ](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=24345174.12688.1.e299c0cb0c558a9c2e7d96ba340bf17e)
---
Check Out What Is Happening At Sheridan.Church!! Watch it here: [https://youtu.be/8KhQu-efqGY](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=24345174.12689.1.9eb51e95e280d2e0d134f265fdd183d9) Please consider giving towards the mission of [Sheridan.Church](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=24345174.12690.1.ddf466fb96b156ff0cd3ce4c1f1c1c5c) by visiting: [www.sheridan.church/give](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=24345174.12691.1.1b42df199d879dcf957765edaf343d88) or mailing a check to 7901 E 41st St Tulsa, OK 74145 Thank you, Pastor Jackson Lahmeyer
---
| | [ Partner w/Sheridan.Church ](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=24345174.12692.1.4328e9b11a783118bebfea11d78c4a14)
---
---
|
---
Recently, I woke up today with a really cool notification... My **FIRST** book, ["_Chasing After The Wind_ "](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=24345174.12693.1.32091b5f51355713c8c9d7a3ec78b8ef) is out and has reached **#12 in Religion/ Christian Living / Personal Growth**! **I have been blown away by the response!** THANK YOU SO MUCH! If you have not ordered your copy you can still get the **Limited Edition Signed Book** here: _[*"Chasing After The Wind"*](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=24345174.12694.1.c3166df2f1ba9420f5a3ac3412191232)_ It is also available on [Amazon](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=24345174.12695.1.03a13b3299d111082ef978e786c4621a)\+ [Barnes & Noble](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=24345174.12696.1.ac7674810d8bbcda51bbc5dc81cb8f92). **Amazon** : [https://www.amazon.com/Chasing-After-Wind-Catching-Everybody/dp/B0F7M4GK26](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=24345174.12697.1.0cc23111d6146cb738a416d5552df2e0) **Barnes & Noble**: [https://www.barnesandnoble.com/w/chasing-after-the-wind-jackson-lahmeyer/1147391019](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=24345174.12698.1.b0868ceb107ffe48426a85b3ef2c83cb) I really want to thank **MLB LEGEND Darryl Strawberry** for writing the Foreword and becoming vulnerable by sharing his story. The Foreword is worth getting the book, I promise. This book is 200 pages and I am confident will change your life as it has mine. In ["Chasing After The Wind"](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=24345174.12699.1.1eb7dd4d97f66761f66f170bb7b28ed0) I share VERY personal stories and I decided to get real and raw with the heartbreaks and triumphs I have experienced.

---
Get your [LIMITED EDITION SIGNED COPY](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=24345174.12701.1.08af0fb41dee2f735548a65202163a4b) here: [https://books.jacksonlahmeyer.com](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=24345174.12702.1.b12e9fb4f16457d2ddccf64fe7798eb5) Thank you,

name: Dan Goldman
subject: A quick recap from around the district
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Dan Goldman for New York**
body:
|
#
---
Peter, During the August recess, I’ve had the opportunity to travel around NY-10 to talk to a wide range of constituents about the issues that matter most to them. When grassroots supporters like you chip in to our campaign, it allows me to spend less time fundraising and more time doing the job you elected me to do: delivering for New Yorkers. **[If you’re able, please consider making a contribution today to keep this work moving forward:](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/114530349/567321641/2132766433?refcode=AU_DSG_FR_GEN_GEN_AC_20250903_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85NDA4NyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiMDdhNzAwMy1kNDg4LWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=smWmXuc8qaj2QjaU2YZuvPvEpu8BxveV1szH-6zqkDE=)** | [DONATE](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/114530350/567321642/2132766433?refcode=AU_DSG_FR_GEN_GEN_AC_20250903_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85NDA4NyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiMDdhNzAwMy1kNDg4LWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=smWmXuc8qaj2QjaU2YZuvPvEpu8BxveV1szH-6zqkDE=)
---
Here’s a look at what I’ve been working on around the district:
**Fighting Trump’s devastating SNAP cuts**
I joined Assemblymember Grace Lee and Councilmember Susan Zhuang to shine a light on Trump’s cruel cuts to SNAP, and the devastating impact they will have on the AAPI community. Trump and Republicans in Congress stripped benefits from more than 300,000 New York households, breaking his promise to make life more affordable for working families and leaving too many New Yorkers hungry.
**Standing up for Social Security**
I rallied with AFGE Local 3369 to push back against Trump and the GOP’s quiet efforts to dismantle Social Security. Nearly 4 million New Yorkers rely on Social Security benefits to make ends meet, and we cannot allow their safety net to be eroded by staff and service cuts that make it impossible to provide benefits to everyone who has earned them.
**Helping kids get ready for the new school year**
I hosted a free back-to-school vaccination and supply drive so children across our district could start the school year healthy, safe, and ready to learn. Every child deserves the resources they need to thrive in the classroom, and I’ll keep working to make sure they have them.
Peter, this is the kind of work I’m most proud of — standing shoulder-to-shoulder with New Yorkers and making sure our community has the resources and protections it needs.
**[Your grassroots support helps me keep my focus where it belongs: on fighting for New Yorkers, not dialing for dollars. If you’re able, please chip in today to support my campaign.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/114530351/567321643/2132766433?refcode=AU_DSG_FR_GEN_GEN_AC_20250903_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85NDA4NyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiMDdhNzAwMy1kNDg4LWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=smWmXuc8qaj2QjaU2YZuvPvEpu8BxveV1szH-6zqkDE=)**
_If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_

clean_body: | Peter, During the August recess, I’ve had the opportunity to travel around NY-10 to talk to a wide range of constituents about the issues that matter most to them. When grassroots supporters like you chip in to our campaign, it allows me to spend less time fundraising and more time doing the job you elected me to do: delivering for New Yorkers. = | = Here’s a look at what I’ve been working on around the district: Fighting Trump’s devastating SNAP cuts I joined Assemblymember Grace Lee and Councilmember Susan Zhuang to shine a light on Trump’s cruel cuts to SNAP, and the devastating impact they will have on the AAPI community. Trump and Republicans in Congress stripped benefits from more than 300,000 New York households, breaking his promise to make life more affordable for working families and leaving too many New Yorkers hungry. Standing up for Social Security I rallied with AFGE Local 3369 to push back against Trump and the GOP’s quiet efforts to dismantle Social Security. Nearly 4 million New Yorkers rely on Social Security benefits to make ends meet, and we cannot allow their safety net to be eroded by staff and service cuts that make it impossible to provide benefits to everyone who has earned them. Helping kids get ready for the new school year I hosted a free back-to-school vaccination and supply drive so children across our district could start the school year healthy, safe, and ready to learn. Every child deserves the resources they need to thrive in the classroom, and I’ll keep working to make sure they have them. Peter, this is the kind of work I’m most proud of — standing shoulder-to-shoulder with New Yorkers and making sure our community has the resources and protections it needs. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | = = | = = | = = Thank you, Dan Dan Goldman for New York PO BOX 3306 New York, NY 10008 United States | mailto:info@dangoldmanforny.com | Paid for by Dan Goldman for New York

[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/114532624/567325939/1793776944?refcode=bsc_emm_ffa_20250903_T1_RuthBenGhiatE2_v1s1&source=bsc_emm_ffa_20250903_T1_RuthBenGhiatE2_v1s1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDMvMS84ODQ1MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3ZDFlNzkzYy1kNjg4LWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=bLpQV2KA6epHoaxAoscXWDRpvQdBlWiYBgtunvJNIp4=&emci=761e793c-d688-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&emdi=7d1e793c-d688-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&ceid=1037277)

| | Team, we’re about to send Professor Ruth Ben-Ghiat this community’s list of questions about defeating autocracy — and we wanted to make sure you had a chance to add yours. [**Submit a question to an expert on how to resist and reverse the autocratic playbook.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/114532625/567325940/-1440599904?refcode=bsc_emm_ffa_20250903_T1_RuthBenGhiatE2_v1s1&source=bsc_emm_ffa_20250903_T1_RuthBenGhiatE2_v1s1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDMvMS84ODQ1MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3ZDFlNzkzYy1kNjg4LWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=bLpQV2KA6epHoaxAoscXWDRpvQdBlWiYBgtunvJNIp4=&emci=761e793c-d688-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&emdi=7d1e793c-d688-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&ceid=1037277) And keep reading for more information on Professor Ben-Ghiat’s work. Thank you, The Fair Fight Action Team \------------------Forwarded Message------------------
From: Lauren Groh-Wargo, Fair Fight Action
Subject: Do you have a question for an expert on tracking and stopping autocracy? You might not think it’s calming to read about autocracy. But I’m here to tell you that when I read the work of Ruth Ben-Ghiat — a Professor of History and Italian Studies at New York University, and an acclaimed expert on authoritarianism and fascism — I feel more settled. That’s because I feel more prepared for the battles ahead. For decades, Professor Ben-Ghiat has studied the tools and practices of autocrats. She’s outlined their playbook in rigorous detail. And when we understand the ins and outs of a playbook, we know how to dismantle it. That’s empowering. So here are a few pieces of recommended reading: Her book _[**Strongmen: Mussolini to the Present**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/114532626/567325941/293958562?refcode=bsc_emm_ffa_20250903_T1_RuthBenGhiatE2_v1s1&source=bsc_emm_ffa_20250903_T1_RuthBenGhiatE2_v1s1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDMvMS84ODQ1MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3ZDFlNzkzYy1kNjg4LWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=bLpQV2KA6epHoaxAoscXWDRpvQdBlWiYBgtunvJNIp4=&emci=761e793c-d688-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&emdi=7d1e793c-d688-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&ceid=1037277)_ (see if your local library has a copy) A recent Substack essay of hers: “[**Is America an Autocracy Now?**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/114532627/567325942/-603584564?refcode=bsc_emm_ffa_20250903_T1_RuthBenGhiatE2_v1s1&source=bsc_emm_ffa_20250903_T1_RuthBenGhiatE2_v1s1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDMvMS84ODQ1MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3ZDFlNzkzYy1kNjg4LWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=bLpQV2KA6epHoaxAoscXWDRpvQdBlWiYBgtunvJNIp4=&emci=761e793c-d688-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&emdi=7d1e793c-d688-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&ceid=1037277)” And another she wrote: “[**What Sen. Booker’s Speech Teaches Us About Effective Resistance Strategy.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/114532628/567325943/377867584?refcode=bsc_emm_ffa_20250903_T1_RuthBenGhiatE2_v1s1&source=bsc_emm_ffa_20250903_T1_RuthBenGhiatE2_v1s1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDMvMS84ODQ1MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3ZDFlNzkzYy1kNjg4LWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=bLpQV2KA6epHoaxAoscXWDRpvQdBlWiYBgtunvJNIp4=&emci=761e793c-d688-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&emdi=7d1e793c-d688-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&ceid=1037277)” Don’t miss this powerful quote from that last piece: _“Authoritarians want us to feel hopeless and helpless. It’s up to us to show them that we will not**submit quietly and that we believe in struggle and collective action.”**_ **To inform our collective fight for democracy, the Fair Fight Action team is gathering questions from our community for Professor Ben-Ghiat. And she’ll be able to answer a few in an upcoming email.** [**Is there something you’d like to ask an expert on tracking and thwarting the plots of would-be dictators? Submit your question for Professor Ruth Ben-Ghiat today.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/114532629/567325944/-1440599904?refcode=bsc_emm_ffa_20250903_T1_RuthBenGhiatE2_v1s1&source=bsc_emm_ffa_20250903_T1_RuthBenGhiatE2_v1s1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDMvMS84ODQ1MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3ZDFlNzkzYy1kNjg4LWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=bLpQV2KA6epHoaxAoscXWDRpvQdBlWiYBgtunvJNIp4=&emci=761e793c-d688-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&emdi=7d1e793c-d688-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&ceid=1037277) And thank you for being on this team. By joining and fueling our shared fight for free and fair elections, you’ve already exercised one of the forms of solidarity that has defeated autocracy in the past — and that will again in the future. It’s on us to defend democracy. And this movement is up to the task. Together, Lauren Groh-Wargo

name: Anika Page
subject: Last Chance: Free Leadership Program for Students
body:
| | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/mspolicy/msla2025-12849880?e=5703353baa)
---
| |
---
| Good morning Friend,
There’s still time to apply for the **Mississippi Leadership Academy (MSLA)** —but only a little. Applications close soon, and we don’t want your student to miss out on this completely **free opportunity** to grow in leadership.
**Why MSLA Matters:**
* Just **one Saturday a month for two months**
* **Volunteer hours signed off** (perfect for college or scholarship applications)
* **Proven impact:** 35% of past students have gone on to intern with leaders they met through our program, including the State Auditor and Attorney General
* **Direct access** to statewide elected officials, legislators, and community leaders
* **Completely free** to apply and participate

This is an investment of only two Saturdays that could open doors for years to come. Encourage your student to apply today before spots are filled!
---
| [ ](https://mspolicy.us18.list-manage.com/track/click?u=60d4052c66b6745831ccdf7c8&id=b9f667db04&e=5703353baa)
---
_Apply by clicking on the photo above!_
| Thank you for helping us raise the next generation of Mississippi leaders.
---
| [Apply now!](https://mspolicy.us18.list-manage.com/track/click?u=60d4052c66b6745831ccdf7c8&id=f0fe434696&e=5703353baa "Apply now!")
---
| |
---
Warmest wishes,

Anika Page
Director of Operations
---
|
---
| | _ _
You are receiving this email because you opted in via our website.

**Our mailing address is:**
Mississippi Center for Public Policy520 George StJackson, MS 39202-3013
[Add us to your address book](https://mspolicy.us18.list-manage.com/vcard?u=60d4052c66b6745831ccdf7c8&id=2f68021ebd)

Want to change how you receive these emails?
You can [update your preferences](https://mcpp-c58f.mailchimpsites.com/manage/preferences?u=60d4052c66b6745831ccdf7c8&id=2f68021ebd&e=5703353baa&c=58392ee2d1) or [unsubscribe from this list](https://mspolicy.us18.list-manage.com/unsubscribe?u=60d4052c66b6745831ccdf7c8&id=2f68021ebd&t=b&e=5703353baa&c=58392ee2d1).

name: The Real Texas PAC
subject: BREAKING: Trump just LOST in court
body:
MORE DATA REQUIRED - SEE BELOW AND PLEASE SHARE YOUR OPINION RIGHT AWAY Donald Trump’s attempt to use the U.S. military as his own personal police force just hit a major roadblock. A federal judge ruled that Trump’s deployment of the National Guard in Los Angeles violated the Posse Comitatus Act — a law that prevents presidents from sending troops into our communities for political gain. This is a MAJOR victory, and we need to make it clear RIGHT AWAY that the American people stand behind this ruling and expect the administration to follow it. That’s why we’re asking you to go on record immediately. Do you support the court’s ruling that Trump’s National Guard deployment in Los Angeles was illegal? Yes, I do No, I do not [https://secure.everyaction.com/QPRDPEj3d062Fmaz_dvjfw2]

subject: Poll: Nonpartisan redistricting commissions
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Integrity First Voter Action Project, www
body:
Voter Action Project [https://voteractionproject.org/]
Opponents of voting rights across the country are working now to use newly gerrymandered seats and new maps to redraw the balance of power in Washington. That's because these vengeful partisans have total control of the process without regard for fairness.
Team: We're looking to hear from our supporters about a way to resolve this. Will you take our one-question poll right away?
Are you in favor of nonpartisan redistricting commissions to draw fair new maps in every state?
By clicking a response below your answer will be automatically recorded:
YES [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-pollresponse?refcode=090325_1yes&amount=15] NO [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-pollresponse?refcode=090325_1no&amount=15]
Voter Action Project [https://voteractionproject.org/]
https://www.facebook.com/voteraction [https://www.facebook.com/voteraction] https://bsky.app/profile/voteractionproject.org [https://bsky.app/profile/voteractionproject.org] https://www.instagram.com/voteractionproj/ [https://www.instagram.com/voteractionproj/] https://www.linkedin.com/company/voter-action-project/ [https://www.linkedin.com/company/voter-action-project/]
Integrity First Voter Action Project is dedicated to expanding ballot access and increasing voter participation across the country.
Since the Supreme Court gutted key provisions in the Voting Rights Act, extreme politicians have taken advantage to pass harsh laws that restrict access to the ballot box. These restrictions particularly affect disadvantaged communities and people of color. It’s our goal to bring awareness to these laws wherever they may crop up and organize voters to take action to ensure the right to vote is available to everyone in their states and communities.
Your support is crucial to our efforts. Will you donate today to help us protect voting rights? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/integrity-first-voter-action-project-1?refcode=090325_1&amount=20]

clean_body: Voter Action Project [] Opponents of voting rights across the country are working now to use newly gerrymandered seats and new maps to redraw the balance of power in Washington. That's because these vengeful partisans have total control of the process without regard for fairness. Team: We're looking to hear from our supporters about a way to resolve this. Will you take our one-question poll right away? Are you in favor of nonpartisan redistricting commissions to draw fair new maps in every state? By clicking a response below your answer will be automatically recorded: YES [] NO [] Voter Action Project [] [] [] [] [] Integrity First Voter Action Project is dedicated to expanding ballot access and increasing voter participation across the country. Since the Supreme Court gutted key provisions in the Voting Rights Act, extreme politicians have taken advantage to pass harsh laws that restrict access to the ballot box. These restrictions particularly affect disadvantaged communities and people of color. It’s our goal to bring awareness to these laws wherever they may crop up and organize voters to take action to ensure the right to vote is available to everyone in their states and communities. Your support is crucial to our efforts. Will you donate today to help us protect voting rights? [] Donate Now [] Contributions or gifts to Integrity First Voter Action Project are not tax deductible as charitable contributions or as business expenses under IRC Section 162(3). # BIG SKY REPORT:
An Update from Senator Daines
---
| Fellow American,

August was an extremely busy month! The month was marked by significant diplomatic achievements, advancements in energy policy, and ongoing efforts to support our communities amid challenges like wildfires and public safety concerns. From brokering international peace to protecting Montana jobs, my team and I worked tirelessly to deliver results for you. Thank you for your continued input and support—it guides everything I do.

I hope you enjoy this August edition of the _**Big Sky Report**_.
---
|
### Policy Achievements: Advancing Montana’s Priorities
#### Brokering Historic Peace Agreement Between Armenia and Azerbaijan
I was proud to join President Trump at the White House on August 8 for the signing of a landmark peace agreement between Azerbaijan and Armenia, along with bilateral deals and the announcement of the Trump Route for International Peace and Prosperity. As Chairman of the European Subcommittee, I traveled to both countries, met with their leaders, and helped facilitate these discussions. This deal enhances U.S. national security by reducing dependency on Russian critical minerals and promotes global stability. [**I previewed this historic moment on Fox News, emphasizing its implications for America.**](http://links.action.supportstevedaines.com/u/click?_t=71faa67ca88f4c4b899697b6a4621901&_m=7250097c47eb4dda9d65bf818cf92baa&_e=Kg5mYKlVxOXTT4JIvb-u5Rp4HBsAs3Q58jPGKMefi4nyCxW6_Buf_L6MA6Dud_SjL8ReRifn-ZGj2RukCTJXa45dEIPG6AHAgQdDCB7QRu2npC0KUPOqG7QZs8l8Nx11EXcFWElAlTuzh9nvPkAA2btnORpJi5wkSnhPD9IY7qcgXLZ7VbzYPOSXXurBNsBCyFh_v31znpfcRZWcZ-wqBgEbrMHyH-zMos2Y6wTsMmBGjp1xWvaWi1DxCUuackCk3IB659jOFYJva__GlNcv311SsylwQc6js4hqDT6_cTf35z7OyolHRxuyFduBt0E9CS8YJ8OSeozXxZ7bQBbcWvnCv8BIbkt0dVR3BdhkDEsSbuxTusLPAltOT7HBnXe2)

[](http://links.action.supportstevedaines.com/u/click?_t=71faa67ca88f4c4b899697b6a4621901&_m=7250097c47eb4dda9d65bf818cf92baa&_e=Kg5mYKlVxOXTT4JIvb-u5Rp4HBsAs3Q58jPGKMefi4nyCxW6_Buf_L6MA6Dud_SjL8ReRifn-ZGj2RukCTJXa-YAJmj4QK9dKlBILDiM57Capaq9KZ6Qi7un6ixwKf-1hkKGE7YQTBXS0kINKMVSthxzc3Cr3hS0vVmC-BRMt-fmxLLdKM5AlbF5S051wWNm25ANHcbEGSpVzAB5g4yMz6TpVcIsnOnfojoXtpQEjHm-kTxRnAmAQhAVeY1C_N7i36b6AGH6FLWRRPuS4-lJVFz2UIAfu3n04p_Je81aU1cGRnPlECm7epitpuMFHKeDc4FZp-FlsHh02LiWHWqaBR13cOYKe1U-AbSwJH8cKMcE_1SQugGK2H-SPo78WTeh)
#### Saving Montana Jobs Through the One Big Beautiful Bill
On August 14, I visited the Bull Mountains Mine near Roundup, which remains operational thanks to a provision I secured in the One Big Beautiful Bill, signed by President Trump. This ensures nearly 250 good-paying Montana jobs and bolsters national energy independence. Media coverage from KTVO highlighted how the mine will stay open for at least nine more years, underscoring our commitment to Made-in-America energy.
#### Expanding Investment Opportunities for American Workers
I applauded President Trump’s Executive Order on August 7, expanding access to alternative assets like private equity and cryptocurrency for workers and retirees with 401(k) plans. Following my earlier letter, I joined colleagues on August 23 in urging the Department of Labor to implement this fully, leveling the playing field for Main Street Americans and enhancing retirement security for Montanans.
#### Protecting Montana’s Energy and Mining Interests
I supported U.S. Forest Service Chief Tom Schultz’s policies to increase logging, mining, and grazing while reducing wildfire risks. Additionally, with Montana’s delegation, I called on President Trump to address Russia’s predatory palladium trade practices, safeguarding jobs at Sibanye-Stillwater in Columbus.
#### Addressing Environmental Concerns at Flathead Lake
Alongside Senator Tim Sheehy, I sent a letter to the Department of the Interior on August 5, urging a long-term solution to decreased water levels at Flathead Lake due to warm conditions and reduced inflows. This is vital for northwestern Montana’s environment, economy, and residents.
---
|
### Community Engagement: Listening to Montanans
#### Visiting Malmstrom Air Force Base
On August 7, I visited Malmstrom Air Force Base to discuss defense wins in the One Big Beautiful Bill, including support for our ICBMs and military priorities. This base is crucial to Montana’s economy and national security, and I’m committed to ensuring our airmen have the resources they need.
#### Responding to Wildfires and Emergencies
I closely monitored the Horn Fire in Madison County on August 14, urging residents to stay safe and thanking firefighters. Following the tragic death of a firefighter battling the Bivens Creek Fire on August 25, Cindy and I prayed for his family and honored his heroism. We also expressed gratitude to first responders after a plane crash at Kalispell City Airport on August 11, where all survived with minor injuries.
#### Celebrating the National Park Service’s Birthday
On August 25, I celebrated the National Park Service’s 109th birthday, highlighting Montana’s treasures like Yellowstone and Glacier National Parks. These are the crown jewels of our state.
---
|
### Looking Ahead
August demonstrated what strong leadership can achieve—from historic international peace deals to protecting Montana’s jobs and natural resources. As we head into fall, I remain focused on delivering for Montana: bolstering our economy, supporting our military and veterans, and addressing community needs. We continue to deliver historic gains for the American people, but we still have a lot of work to do. [**If you want to be a part of this fight, please consider making a contribution of any amount to my re-election campaign.**](http://links.action.supportstevedaines.com/u/click?_t=71faa67ca88f4c4b899697b6a4621901&_m=7250097c47eb4dda9d65bf818cf92baa&_e=Kg5mYKlVxOXTT4JIvb-u5dG9Vga9gU8zQs8FjpvG7jALMDwCDyyeQN_mgeWMzWU4b7nAm9HI-QvVRdP9zOglM-oLqaOobUweduC-nKpBtiXDLsdKAXAguwogw94cLJsaYrqKlzivXaIUjivMjnQKdSaNmVtU3rpAUqSUVmWTtDivEakMBwd7UbGJnU-uRg0Ct4v9__zIbXUXoHSTy8qlTuD25MHtplmcPitfi_Avo0JaPt3QfQP5hi0CdDfPqJTmkYGbJ7ozFQkGscnq3L7VHtpqNvgGZsODG7KcV28FTJ04Sy8X9Xol46jVoljH6Q4guPyMMwPOPkA0b26Zam_al5tPpq2M1pGlOu1Q25q-kei3BoOZVFciZHIcKFyoYbv8QOeuC_gIPCMfOoLuBVyyB6qC-j8mwyZRvDZnrKfgQ-RRui4fUgikuZkn_PR30UYZquI1IryJsFqwmY0e1KnB0A4S0NFhH2LeaWWcyKDke7ySCoFeT2riEJyb4XIgflYEAvQiU-BZ6OGSi5uZ63r48Qkdu4o2QKN9uK3cH1_or4lCcZVPYQpes6lvivPWDHnjj4uoJi0ZyWW_QF1ojobeG5LaMbiKSj3KUhKKcuCKwc4QgAqNvq6Krda1eezZg5-Mh4QAo6b-Qco6bF-4s1zpYkXVP6abVSiyOM-I9e-TzjUk8n-Sl3Jdep47PpHwW1umFy4R1bmVgoIj5-cyMW9v_sJKkOV82HbZ6NOAlSBIOsyYFqrMIJuaAPCxVbOyzQrJSD7P1WALopBXdfT3r7CjBg%3D%3D)

#### ABOUT SENATOR STEVE DAINES
_Steve Daines is a fifth-generation Montanan, life-long sportsman and father of four serving the people of Montana as their Senator. Steve is committed to always putting Montana first as he works to bring real change to Washington and fights for more jobs, less government and common sense solutions to get our country back on track._

Expanding Investment Opportunities for American Workers I applauded President Trump’s Executive Order on August 7, expanding access to alternative assets like private equity and cryptocurrency for workers and retirees with 401(k) plans. Following my earlier letter, I joined colleagues on August 23 in urging the Department of Labor to implement this fully, leveling the playing field for Main Street Americans and enhancing retirement security for Montanans. Protecting Montana’s Energy and Mining Interests I supported U.S. Forest Service Chief Tom Schultz’s policies to increase logging, mining, and grazing while reducing wildfire risks. Additionally, with Montana’s delegation, I called on President Trump to address Russia’s predatory palladium trade practices, safeguarding jobs at Sibanye-Stillwater in Columbus. Addressing Environmental Concerns at Flathead Lake Alongside Senator Tim Sheehy, I sent a letter to the Department of the Interior on August 5, urging a long-term solution to decreased water levels at Flathead Lake due to warm conditions and reduced inflows. This is vital for northwestern Montana’s environment, economy, and residents. | Community Engagement: Listening to Montanans Visiting Malmstrom Air Force Base On August 7, I visited Malmstrom Air Force Base to discuss defense wins in the One Big Beautiful Bill, including support for our ICBMs and military priorities. This base is crucial to Montana’s economy and national security, and I’m committed to ensuring our airmen have the resources they need. Responding to Wildfires and Emergencies I closely monitored the Horn Fire in Madison County on August 14, urging residents to stay safe and thanking firefighters. Following the tragic death of a firefighter battling the Bivens Creek Fire on August 25, Cindy and I prayed for his family and honored his heroism. We also expressed gratitude to first responders after a plane crash at Kalispell City Airport on August 11, where all survived with minor injuries. Celebrating the National Park Service’s Birthday On August 25, I celebrated the National Park Service’s 109th birthday, highlighting Montana’s treasures like Yellowstone and Glacier National Parks. These are the crown jewels of our state. | Looking Ahead August demonstrated what strong leadership can achieve—from historic international peace deals to protecting Montana’s jobs and natural resources. As we head into fall, I remain focused on delivering for Montana: bolstering our economy, supporting our military and veterans, and addressing community needs. We continue to deliver historic gains for the American people, but we still have a lot of work to do. For Montana, Senator Steve Daines | ABOUT SENATOR STEVE DAINES Steve Daines is a fifth-generation Montanan, life-long sportsman and father of four serving the people of Montana as their Senator. Steve is committed to always putting Montana first as he works to bring real change to Washington and fights for more jobs, less government and common sense solutions to get our country back on track. Paid for by Steve Daines for Montana

name: Adam Schwarze
subject: A message from a regular guy
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Schwarze for Senate
body:
Your past support has helped Republicans win tough battles, and we need you now to introduce me to Minnesota voters | [](http://links.freedomfirstalert.com/u/click?_t=9ca143e971904d26a973d002cb0ae7f7&_m=db1af2afc8164cae89528e82d11065c7&_e=tXIBmRFwMon_3opDsa_xMrxnDQLQff24XalepuecHjjuqahKhy-DZvlvFArctWhfHJR53rugV8lt8xHnnk-lXaxtbyhad55QyBUgl_FhYQQXPhVDDASkoDF4gpamHYhpixaNonwmPQ3h9zaCL_beOkhMWhSIGPQb0x-8RlFzOtzO1Z4Qg6_Ep4Z_xiW1-Z03NasUcLoPqnh3FtVYNekwEOJZN1GA6fyRIoZ0zrlr-r8cUj5mO9EuDTisFRizW1hwKHEEOCO_nq62ShKsP2GKCUVB4_nhNO8DPQwHyt4MsBYdEa6gj6vTyOcwxuX5T8SAfU2QfabbALoWbvyvFyOQ4F2IIUbX5Iy2yWqJCiTO0FrLJNAqzNFHDIzpcBIV7KRHjYinUYiD3XfjmkDEnN1PyBLUBQBIKAkP9VzNHI3Sr8a9YiMLIr9nRUlM440Aq9CGruynOK2WLL-XKCa9xAePV9tvgbNj7VPAc-xB-vLrevBvIvDUD_1oK3hIwpkb7iOJnWffTZgEw12R5op0yVwdISvrN_I5_eSZDO_TPeW5vdMJUv2HpYb7t_fGIWePdbFABHpXi4FMIfu3P64KGOSLazTStAOIjzzqZ3x4hsP0wWwdkJw02EYr-o9mY3YOwV8RA_jcCXj2elbzNhx3UwbsFVxDt4BzK11tkAqC6DId9IezfUzcwnyeUIJq_ZckMpydo-Gziem_A9KOOrsKJNHwa_48cOqIZ9hJdqehE7vBAXGRQ5ctjFo3K_6k5zWdwYAhwuggZ2Xbt7JPjOUdgKL-cw%3D%3D)

---
Fellow Conservative,

You may not know me yet, but my name is Adam Schwarze, and I’m a lifelong Minnesotan, former Navy SEAL, and Marine Corps veteran running for U.S. Senate in 2026. I’m stepping into this fight because Minnesota—and our country—needs a new voice to stand up for our values, and I’m asking for your help to make it happen.

I grew up in Bloomington, Minnesota, playing hockey and learning teamwork from my working-class parents. _**On my 18th birthday in October 2001, just weeks after 9/11, I enlisted in the Marine Corps to serve our nation.**_

**Over 21 years, I deployed nine times to over 70 countries as a Marine and Navy SEAL, putting my life on the line to defend our freedoms.** I earned my undergraduate degree from the University of Minnesota, a Master’s from Harvard, a Master’s Certificate from Johns Hopkins in Security and Nuclear Deterrence, and an MBA. But at my core, I’m a regular guy who wants to make my mom proud and fight for the Minnesota I love.

Right now, Minnesota and America face a critical moment. The radical policies of Tim Walz and his lieutenant governor—who’s running to replace retiring Senator Tina Smith—threaten our way of life. From the unrest in Minneapolis in 2020 to attacks on our police, their agenda doesn’t reflect the Minnesota I know. _**This open Senate seat in 2026 is our chance to take back our state and stop the left’s push toward extremism. Just like in Georgia, where one seat flipped the Senate majority in 2021, Minnesota’s vote could decide the future of the U.S. Senate.

**_ **But here’s the challenge: I’m a new face, and the Democrats are already raising millions to keep this seat.** I’ve noticed you haven’t been active lately, Fellow Conservative, and I get it—the news can feel overwhelming, and it’s easy to think the fight’s already won. But it’s not. Right now, donations are slowing, and the other side is **quietly building its war chest**. I’m thinking of patriots like you who’ve supported conservatives before. Your past support has helped Republicans win tough battles, and we need you now to introduce me to Minnesota voters.

clean_body: Your past support has helped Republicans win tough battles, and we need you now to introduce me to Minnesota voters | Fellow Conservative, You may not know me yet, but my name is Adam Schwarze, and I’m a lifelong Minnesotan, former Navy SEAL, and Marine Corps veteran running for U.S. Senate in 2026. I’m stepping into this fight because Minnesota—and our country—needs a new voice to stand up for our values, and I’m asking for your help to make it happen. I grew up in Bloomington, Minnesota, playing hockey and learning teamwork from my working-class parents. On my 18th birthday in October 2001, just weeks after 9/11, I enlisted in the Marine Corps to serve our nation. Over 21 years, I deployed nine times to over 70 countries as a Marine and Navy SEAL, putting my life on the line to defend our freedoms. I earned my undergraduate degree from the University of Minnesota, a Master’s from Harvard, a Master’s Certificate from Johns Hopkins in Security and Nuclear Deterrence, and an MBA. But at my core, I’m a regular guy who wants to make my mom proud and fight for the Minnesota I love. Right now, Minnesota and America face a critical moment. The radical policies of Tim Walz and his lieutenant governor—who’s running to replace retiring Senator Tina Smith—threaten our way of life. From the unrest in Minneapolis in 2020 to attacks on our police, their agenda doesn’t reflect the Minnesota I know. This open Senate seat in 2026 is our chance to take back our state and stop the left’s push toward extremism. Just like in Georgia, where one seat flipped the Senate majority in 2021, Minnesota’s vote could decide the future of the U.S. Senate. But here’s the challenge: I’m a new face, and the Democrats are already raising millions to keep this seat. I’ve noticed you haven’t been active lately, Fellow Conservative, and I get it—the news can feel overwhelming, and it’s easy to think the fight’s already won. But it’s not. Right now, donations are slowing, and the other side is quietly building its war chest. I’m thinking of patriots like you who’ve supported conservatives before. Your past support has helped Republicans win tough battles, and we need you now to introduce me to Minnesota voters. It’s not about the amount—it’s a signal that you’re with me, that you believe Minnesota and America deserve a conservative fighter who’s served our nation and will serve you in Washington. As a Navy SEAL, I’ve thrived in the most challenging missions and am ready for this campaign. But I can’t do it without you. We need thousands of grassroots supporters, not Hollywood elites or mega-donors like the Democrats rely on. Minnesota hasn’t voted Republican for president since 1976, but this race is winnable. I’m running to secure our borders, support law enforcement, revive our economy, and protect our freedoms. Please, chip in $5 today to help me introduce myself to Minnesotans and fight for our shared values. | | Dear Fellow Patriot,**Did Barack Obama commit treason when he launched a multi-front campaign to destroy Donald Trump, his allies, and cripple his incoming Administration?** My answer is “Yes” because I was personally and professionally destroyed by them.Barack Obama along with Hillary Clinton, Susan Rice, James Comey, and other power-hungry radicals concocted this scheme from inside the White House.But the real conspiracy wasn’t just behind closed doors.Everyone was in on it – especially and most dangerously the mainstream media. I’m talking about 24/7 hit pieces. The never-ending barrage of mysterious leaks. Their conspiracy was made possible by collaborators inside the FBI like James Comey and the House Intelligence Committee like Adam Schiff who lied to your face that they had evidence to “get” Trump.This is why I’ve partnered with my friends at the American Media Council to make sure we can reshape the media landscape before the next hoax can destroy our country. **[Today, I’m asking you to join me in supporting their critical mission.](http://links.conservativesfordjt.com/u/click?_t=05b03ef2209247bcbdad7fd5099cd670&_m=28ac31662c3244ea83efea57ea96edaa&_e=InoeXeKz32iMNDvJl9I4JLp0bGTA3cm4PHjBm90HHvrjHmKOZNqyDfiF_82I_N-mEg5SyKlJX-OFPfSIYGSDwVLkrGnWD06jYslDKa-sGCaxrS7bV3gi_MVQBNxOmIx9jpcV0qv2NOZADjabwvwW1cMUieRblf7A5JFfxvOhzSfsBpTs1hDHyvpMZsp1TkjMFBRt7cCN96kj_EwL6vu6e7LUX5Qqg4d7cnSjqeeZn71bVu6oUecE-TKl-_vxhWOsAie7sjHvO9JobxXV3ZOkVhv7s1P5fHqYcxOBebiLZ89hP7jn2SHM1AiQ5TuaqFWBcwHmWdLR1EZODhiJeLy6SBewUsf8ulCpbmwufGKe2VilTHzrkeodpM4LQkkZPBIXD9t_zl5mm4b4_Bxhz3J1ahbkxvwXZM3cfplpwPnvKSVSPuXMZuanAH2AFFu0VmC3PVLI0k2ZXPDmhV-IVLnZ3_FPEyxhZFAYcmKdT3sbMgqMEJxXGZtF9wGmuP2K_vyDBKtH3arGtt_ND2qYeqkLG1JCJXa9KxCYsEhyK7Cj1tqBlrkJJpCkCdl68jhWymT0SApqErpWz8qF181ltXHqQYPD-p8JoF2P6zh6zwc9oJ3JEiINuVnNvLuj3ML1paQYwg7rnpoAqYTi3bm4l70nSgBw3acdq_Cvipd2O-ZABuDbTpgVlUt6gp31LqXDZ5qmKfCy4jtszN4q7qF4g3cFjPp6Vw1f8I4ZYjdgX3S_rw8V_DqBqriYxGsx223cqUj8L7KBrd_MH9jRjNorpx_T_w%3D%3D)** It’s a mission I care deeply about because I was the target of the Fake News’ lies.Here’s the truth: Obama and Hillary didn’t have a lick of evidence when they cooked up this hoax. Here’s the _disturbing_ truth: they didn’t need any.They could make up whatever they wanted to and feed it to the mainstream media monster. That’s exactly what they did.Let me explain:
---
1. They planted fake evidence in the mainstream media.

2. The mainstream media ran them as if they were fact.

3. The mainstream media's coverage was cited as proof the fake evidence was real.

---
It was terrible circular logic built on a mountain of lies.**But it worked.** It worked because the liberal media drilled it into the minds of millions of Americans that President Trump was some kind of traitor.The whole time, these Trump-hating journalists celebrated their role in making this hoax a reality. The Washington Post even won a Pulitzer Prize over it!The level to which the liberal media went to justify this insanity was insane itself.That’s why today I’m honored to partner with a group who’s fighting to stop another hoax like this from destroying our country.The American Media Council understands what’s really going on:**The power-hungry radicals in Washington who have weaponized our government have also weaponized the mainstream media.** Their mission is to restore truth in the media before the Fake News can destroy our country. They’re the reason I’m able to write you this. What I deeply respect about the American Media Council is that they aren’t sitting back and watching it happen. They’re on the frontlines trying to make a difference.
---
* They publish the facts behind top media hoaxes.
* They hold journalists accountable with their “Truth in Journalism” Pledge.
* They give out “Trustworthy Ratings” to help patriots like you know which sites you can trust.

---
But that’s not all.Thanks to the generous support of patriots like you, they have a **“First Amendment Fund”** to help conservatives overcome lawfare waged by the mainstream media. **[Today, will you consider filling their “First Amendment Fund” with a donation of $35 or more?](http://links.conservativesfordjt.com/u/click?_t=05b03ef2209247bcbdad7fd5099cd670&_m=28ac31662c3244ea83efea57ea96edaa&_e=InoeXeKz32iMNDvJl9I4JLp0bGTA3cm4PHjBm90HHvrjHmKOZNqyDfiF_82I_N-mEg5SyKlJX-OFPfSIYGSDwVLkrGnWD06jYslDKa-sGCaxrS7bV3gi_MVQBNxOmIx9jpcV0qv2NOZADjabwvwW1cMUieRblf7A5JFfxvOhzSfsBpTs1hDHyvpMZsp1TkjMFBRt7cCN96kj_EwL6vu6e7LUX5Qqg4d7cnSjqeeZn71bVu6oUecE-TKl-_vxhWOsAie7sjHvO9JobxXV3ZOkVikoHdNrxpqq6Q9TvWOsbIjnUpchyBCTAaOoglKJs22ixS3_u4G9U9a5RUPBlDT_gj7NMRb3RyvUjwcGZyDRNhxC-hmHngi4x604oJ7wdgEynluKItKTBZilQFbg-C3dRsUiX2xWIbBnch9aWvJNmL2SFRCO1sXxISG4WER2Y7c30F9t4BH-RuQ2Cnc1rupNJ54sjHNHRV5_g_xA-5xJWk_x4j_JHLxwXeISCtbF6A80kWIXXR2WMkpPYFmjkntv-xzaH-L9dWnNHENAILfa_-sP8qi_1MzV7NtL9PHoMWEAWOo_87_b53ZcDSeIwvtKEk5dG6HXg03IUp8mdJbPUt6ZT75c01b2LADo1yhF2RFCl-0V2VE87q3XYcRd-ktH410rAu4X-uCqowGr_CNjwVK6iQdHpjYM4-uO35d77VlhGVOpMK-4fheJtVnsIpoz7OgBpCrkCNs7GTJxRyFmqk5VYDyl7lnDWinX8F8_epdHdKy9xIJYZMKNwL0l9JSDFQ%3D%3D)** A $35 donation would be a tremendous help. But if you feel called to make a monthly donation, say $15 or more, I will send you a complimentary copy of Pardon of Innocence.It’s so important to me that you support the American Media Council that I want to “Thank You” with a complimentary book.My friend, it’s hard to put into words just how impactful your support will be today...... but I’ll try.The Deep State wanted to destroy me.They tried to take away my career and financially bankrupt my family. I had to sell my house to pay the endless legal fees. It put my wife, Lori, through hell and back.That’s the Left’s goal.**Humiliation. Retaliation. Destruction.**Sadly, the damage to my reputation can’t be fixed in a courtroom or wiped from the internet.President Donald Trump pardoned me. My name has been officially cleared. But no matter where I go... certain people will always see me the way the liberal media painted me.That’s why the Left pushes these hoaxes. It’s all about inflicting **maximum punishment** on anybody who dares to stand up against their radical doctrine.Your support of the American Media Council and their **“First Amendment Fund”** today will directly help patriots like me clear their names and stand up against the liberal media’s lies.This is only possible with your support today. **[Will you support the American Media Council just like I do?](http://links.conservativesfordjt.com/u/click?_t=05b03ef2209247bcbdad7fd5099cd670&_m=28ac31662c3244ea83efea57ea96edaa&_e=InoeXeKz32iMNDvJl9I4JLp0bGTA3cm4PHjBm90HHvrjHmKOZNqyDfiF_82I_N-mEg5SyKlJX-OFPfSIYGSDwVLkrGnWD06jYslDKa-sGCaxrS7bV3gi_MVQBNxOmIx9jpcV0qv2NOZADjabwvwW1cMUieRblf7A5JFfxvOhzSfsBpTs1hDHyvpMZsp1TkjMFBRt7cCN96kj_EwL6vu6e7LUX5Qqg4d7cnSjqeeZn71bVu6oUecE-TKl-_vxhWOsAie7sjHvO9JobxXV3ZOkVim8r86lHJOAny7c2gxOrzNH4nFzbwMcRYE5dPX2lTvkz7RjrJoRad3S4sscZee_JrXkmNwiJYcrel3jPiIQsVyoB7tB3ZtqnuxoQw99u5D_VfO309MFKXZRM9lYCWd-tPf9FhagnaodyAENl8iRQmq8wzhqn2QSZ8z35y9VPxih1IDrLq-JUtb7qsCjRyBKHBBbKwcGTShsQRrNX923R9UE9WkLyVYsXY0-C0m6u0LOCIqCKcDGy8gsxuIQH1hrj_uvC7imQHaaUceI50c40H5xSP2xpwuA4H0OoynF6AXaI6wDGQ0bPWsHw1TwjujxNXn4TdzCQ6ltZOBb0sKX_XUhzLcNVQq6bHVy1EyRJEV-FZckCTCPugmXgrXyG83opZidqJmA6ytjTmxapTr_qn3gJNgsZKfSnPAWDa42WhpASkRobmaZdgoywNICZkWLVoJmGPqOYm4TAUU5h3H9pnWf77LiiKnsnlrfPLwBZAojHFZjC0sSsctexvNPuntDLw%3D%3D)** If you’re in a position to sign up for a monthly donation today – $15 or more – I will send you a complimentary copy of Pardon of Innocence as my sincere thanks.
---
Thank You and God Bless You
---

# [7 Lessons Learned From Working Over 40 Jobs](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=4399133&post_id=172689257&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTcyNjg5MjU3LCJpYXQiOjE3NTY5MTM3MTksImV4cCI6MTc1OTUwNTcxOSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTQzOTkxMzMiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.TNZI2pmB5oVQI65MOsLpmK0IQqCHEoq2nLhS_i6Pz_I)
### What delivering newspapers, waiting tables, and practicing law taught me about the dignity of work
| | [Patrick J. Murphy](https://substack.com/@patrickmurphypa)
---
| Sep 3
---
| [](https://substack.com/@patrickmurphypa)
---

---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=4399133&post_id=172689257&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTcyNjg5MjU3LCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTc1NjkxMzcxOSwiZXhwIjoxNzU5NTA1NzE5LCJpc3MiOiJwdWItNDM5OTEzMyIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.FnvfNlMNYqOu1vmfn6SJx2JilsLd42aJBrsKKcUOPME&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=4399133&post_id=172689257&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=true&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTcyNjg5MjU3LCJpYXQiOjE3NTY5MTM3MTksImV4cCI6MTc1OTUwNTcxOSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTQzOTkxMzMiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.TNZI2pmB5oVQI65MOsLpmK0IQqCHEoq2nLhS_i6Pz_I&r=1hvr60&utm_campaign=email-half-magic-comments&action=post-comment&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)

| | [](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9vcGVuLnN1YnN0YWNrLmNvbS9wdWIvcGF0cmlja211cnBoeXBhL3AvNy1sZXNzb25zLWxlYXJuZWQtZnJvbS13b3JraW5nLW92ZXI_.It7NBIeaA9UacS6nBh9O-b2gNDc2xu5IzvfwxAHms24?&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/patrickmurphypa/p/7-lessons-learned-from-working-over?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---

President Ronald Reagan famously said, "A job is the best social program there is." I think about that line every Labor Day.
It's hard to believe that just five years ago, at the height of the pandemic, unemployment in America hit **[14.8%](https://substack.com/redirect/f9d7bbbf-9bd3-4cc6-8eb3-a9e0c2458880?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** --the highest level since the government started collecting data in 1948. As the virus started spreading, [ten million jobs](https://substack.com/redirect/40c64a0f-b209-49e5-b694-12445c61c666?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) disappeared almost overnight, and the idea of full-time employment fell out of reach for many. Eventually, though, things turned around. Today, America enjoys one of the lowest unemployment rates of any industrialized nation at **4.2%**.
[For perspective](https://substack.com/redirect/3f2b2df4-f4a8-484f-8550-a027792838e4?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg): the U.K. is at 4.7%, and France is around 7%. Germany, though, has us beat at 3.7%. In other words, America--despite all its flaws--remains one of the best places on Earth to find work.
Having a job is about more than just work, though. It's about dignity. And the dignity of work is something we should never take for granted.
I've been blessed to have had a strong work ethic instilled in me since I was 9, first delivering papers for _The Carrier Pigeon_(yes, [that's a real publication](https://substack.com/redirect/b4999668-3087-4764-b1d7-8511828e004f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) advertising local businesses) and then for the _Philadelphia Inquirer_. My older brother JJ and I had 84 heavy Sunday editions to lug around. Instead of breaking our backs lugging them around, we got creative. We stacked the papers in a shopping cart we borrowed from the local Acme, laced up our roller skates, and pushed the carts down each city block as we divided and conquered.
That was **my first lesson** in efficiency: work smarter, not harder.
The Secretary's SitRep is a reader-supported publication. To receive new posts and support my work, consider becoming a free or paid subscriber.
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..GvRDZ0jLk_2hpUbq6UU7AAM4FWMsvoCxHDPL988m5RQ?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget-preamble&utm_content=172689257)
# **Lesson #2 Embrace the Suck**
I discovered pretty quickly that being in front of people came naturally to me--a skill that would later serve me well when I ran for office. I started in the food industry as a cook and dishwasher at the local Pizza Hut in the Philadelphia Mills mall. It was a fine job for a fifteen-year-old, provided you didn't mind smelling like pepperoni all day. But the real thrill came when I moved up to busboy at the local Italian Bistro. Suddenly, there were tips. The more I hustled, the more I made. The idea of earning tips for my work was highly motivating.
The tips got even better the following year when I took on the "graveyard shift" of waiting tables, 11 p.m. to 6 a.m., for the Perkins family restaurant.
That job taught me to "embrace the suck."
An overnight shift serving pancakes to intoxicated customers is one kind of suck; a month of 130-degree heat without proper plumbing in Iraq during the ["Fire Month"](https://substack.com/redirect/f908204f-fd6e-42ed-88a3-ef12f6f554e4?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) of August 2003 is another. Both teach you how to grit your teeth, tough it out, and keep moving forward with a smile but stern look when needed.
# **Lesson #3 The Subtle Art of Suggestion**
By the time I landed at Olive Garden years later, I thought I'd seen all the restaurant world had to offer. But Olive Garden had its own peculiar system, complete with a ranked system of employee performance. Waiters were ranked not just on service, but on "add-ons"--the appetizers, bottles of wine, and desserts we could tack on to an order.
The trick was never to ask, "Do you want dessert?" No one wants to be the person who raises their hand for cheesecake after having pasta and never-ending breadsticks. Instead, you brought over the tray of sweets, including chocolate cake, lemon meringue, tiramisu--an entire pageant of sugary treats. Faced with this glossy display, at least one person at the table would always surrender to their sweet tooth. It was less a sales pitch than a Jedi mind trick.
I mastered the subtle art of suggestion through showing, not telling.
It may seem trivial in the grand picture of my life, but being ranked number one at the Olive Garden out of dozens of other waiters is something I'll always be proud of.
# **The lesson here (#4): treat all work with the respect it deserves.**
It doesn't matter how big or small the task is.
When I was 18, my parents moved to Hershey, PA, and I worked yet another graveyard shift at the Hershey Reese's factory. They offered fifty cents more an hour for overnight work, and to me, that was a no-brainer. I could pull all-nighters at the factory and still lifeguard during the day that summer before I joined Army ROTC later that fall.
It was hard, exhausting work, but it was also phenomenal. I was surrounded by people who were salt of the Earth folks and as good as the peanut butter cups we packed and the KitKats we baked. I was basically Willy Wonka that summer.
## **Lesson #5 Stay Humble**
Eventually, I moved on to more buttoned-up work. When I earned my J.D., being proactive instead of reactive was something I learned early on in the law. In America, we don't really have one justice system--we have two. One for people who can afford lawyers, and another for those just trying to get by without one. That's why I was proud to serve on the board of the Legal Services Corporation, which funds more than 200 legal aid clinics across the country.
But my start was humble. I didn't attend a prestigious law school that earns you a Supreme Court clerkship or plush job in Manhattan or DC. I attended Widener Commonwealth University School of Law in Harrisburg, Pennsylvania--a school most of you probably haven't heard of. It wasn't fancy, but I earned a great education. While there, I became the student clinic leader at the Harrisburg Civil Law Clinic under Professor Palmer Lockard. Our ragtag team of law students helped anyone who walked through the door: a single mom who needed a will, a retiree fighting off a fraudulent lawsuit, someone who just needed help filling out a job application.
At the same time, I was president of the St. Thomas More Society, which volunteered at the [St. Francis soup kitchen](https://substack.com/redirect/06a32df9-c02a-46ac-b085-a7abd6d9492a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). What struck me most was how often the people we served hot meals to were the very same people who needed legal help, but had no idea the law clinic even existed a few miles away.
So we brought the law to them. Once a month, we set up a table in the soup kitchen and offered legal services right there. It was one of the most gratifying experiences of my career. To sit across from someone, look them in the eyes, and give them even the smallest measure of hope--hope that someone was willing to fight for them--was the kind of job that stays with you.
# **Lesson #6 Bigger isn 't Always Better**
If you're someone who attends one of the elite Law Schools, first of all, congrats. Chances are, you are considering a career in Big Law, which is understandable since the starting salary for a first-year associate is over $200k in the major markets. But beware that Big Law is not nearly as glamorous as people think. There are more than 50,000 law firms in America, but the top 100--the so-called _Am Law 100_ --are treated like a league of their own. (They're called "white-shoe firms," a term so pretentious it needed its own vocabulary.)
I know from experience. I've worked at two of them.
After five years of active duty in the JAG Corps, where I tried cases before federal court and military martials, I knew I was ready for the big leagues.
After months of interviews and being unemployed (having turned down the first job I was offered), I joined Cozen O'Connor, a first-generation mid-size Big Law firm co-founded by Patrick O'Connor, a fellow King's College graduate. The O'Connor family was practically its own legal dynasty. I had worked for Michael O'Connor as a messenger when I was 14, and for Tom O'Connor--who later became a judge--while I was at King's College. To finally work for Pat, one of the true legal titans of his generation, felt like a full-circle moment.
The reality was less glamorous. There were nights I literally slept in the office and shaved in the bathroom with razors I picked up at the local 7-Eleven. It wasn't what I pictured when I imagined practicing at a major firm. But I was home, back in Philadelphia after years away, and working alongside some of the best lawyers in the country was truly an honor. I also met the mother of my children there.
For a skinny kid from Northeast Philly who started full-time at community college, it was an opportunity I'll never forget, and one I'll always be grateful the O'Connor brothers gave me.
# **Lesson #7 The Best Jobs Aren 't Jobs At All**
The greatest job I'll ever have is being a dad. It's cliche, but true. If you read my piece _[This #GirlDad Says Goodbye](https://substack.com/redirect/9f06d671-ffcc-4121-8961-995488d5901e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_, you know how emotional I was about my daughter Maggie starting college at Clemson and joining ROTC. That's how close we are. And I'm just as close to my 15-year-old son, Jack.
Being a parent is a never-ending job that involves sacrifice. But it's always worth it.
There were jobs and opportunities I turned down so I could coach Jack in hockey year after year. I'll never forget leaving the Pentagon late at night, driving straight through until morning just to make one of his tournaments in Canada. Maggie, meanwhile, was a competitive dancer for over a decade before switching to high school volleyball. I made it to almost every recital and every game. I don't regret a single missed professional opportunity I turned down for them.
But the best part of all those years wasn't the tournaments or the recitals, it was the rides to and from practice. The rule was simple: whoever sat in the front seat couldn't be on their phone. We had to talk. Those conversations are what I'll treasure most. Letting them DJ the music, on the other hand, is one thing I don't.
I know as those rides become few and far between, the bond will carry on for decades. Those were some of our most heartfelt conversations.
I've had so many jobs over the years, it's hard to keep track. But what they all have in common is this: a job is never just a job. It's a story and a lesson. Sometimes it's humiliation, sometimes it's a small triumph. Sometimes a huge, legacy-defining triumph. But whatever it is, every job reveals something deeper about yourself that you carry with you into the next chapter of your life.
So, as people go back to school or return to work from summer vacation, remember your job doesn't solely define you. But the sum of your experiences do shape who you are. Each experience deserves its own measure of pride.
And yes, not to be overly sentimental, but being a dad is the greatest job I've ever had and the title I'll always be proudest to hold.
-- Patrick

clean_body: What delivering newspapers, waiting tables, and practicing law taught me about the dignity of work | | ---|---|--- | | | Forwarded this email? ? for more ? Thank you for supporting my work. If my writing resonates with you, consider . Annual paid subscribers will receive a signed copy of my 2008 memoir, What delivering newspapers, waiting tables, and practicing law taught me about the dignity of work | | | Sep 3 | | | | | | | | | | | | President Ronald Reagan famously said, "A job is the best social program there is." I think about that line every Labor Day. It's hard to believe that just five years ago, at the height of the pandemic, unemployment in America hit --the highest level since the government started collecting data in 1948. As the virus started spreading, disappeared almost overnight, and the idea of full-time employment fell out of reach for many. Eventually, though, things turned around. Today, America enjoys one of the lowest unemployment rates of any industrialized nation at 4.2%. : the U.K. is at 4.7%, and France is around 7%. Germany, though, has us beat at 3.7%. In other words, America--despite all its flaws--remains one of the best places on Earth to find work. Having a job is about more than just work, though. It's about dignity. And the dignity of work is something we should never take for granted. I've been blessed to have had a strong work ethic instilled in me since I was 9, first delivering papers for The Carrier Pigeon(yes, advertising local businesses) and then for the Philadelphia Inquirer. My older brother JJ and I had 84 heavy Sunday editions to lug around. Instead of breaking our backs lugging them around, we got creative. We stacked the papers in a shopping cart we borrowed from the local Acme, laced up our roller skates, and pushed the carts down each city block as we divided and conquered. That was my first lesson in efficiency: work smarter, not harder. The Secretary's SitRep is a reader-supported publication. To receive new posts and support my work, consider becoming a free or paid subscriber. Lesson #2 Embrace the Suck I discovered pretty quickly that being in front of people came naturally to me--a skill that would later serve me well when I ran for office. I started in the food industry as a cook and dishwasher at the local Pizza Hut in the Philadelphia Mills mall. It was a fine job for a fifteen-year-old, provided you didn't mind smelling like pepperoni all day. But the real thrill came when I moved up to busboy at the local Italian Bistro. Suddenly, there were tips. The more I hustled, the more I made. The idea of earning tips for my work was highly motivating. The tips got even better the following year when I took on the "graveyard shift" of waiting tables, 11 p.m. to 6 a.m., for the Perkins family restaurant. That job taught me to "embrace the suck." An overnight shift serving pancakes to intoxicated customers is one kind of suck; a month of 130-degree heat without proper plumbing in Iraq during the of August 2003 is another. Both teach you how to grit your teeth, tough it out, and keep moving forward with a smile but stern look when needed. Lesson #3 The Subtle Art of Suggestion By the time I landed at Olive Garden years later, I thought I'd seen all the restaurant world had to offer. But Olive Garden had its own peculiar system, complete with a ranked system of employee performance. Waiters were ranked not just on service, but on "add-ons"--the appetizers, bottles of wine, and desserts we could tack on to an order. The trick was never to ask, "Do you want dessert?" No one wants to be the person who raises their hand for cheesecake after having pasta and never-ending breadsticks. Instead, you brought over the tray of sweets, including chocolate cake, lemon meringue, tiramisu--an entire pageant of sugary treats. Faced with this glossy display, at least one person at the table would always surrender to their sweet tooth. It was less a sales pitch than a Jedi mind trick. I mastered the subtle art of suggestion through showing, not telling. It may seem trivial in the grand picture of my life, but being ranked number one at the Olive Garden out of dozens of other waiters is something I'll always be proud of. The lesson here (#4): treat all work with the respect it deserves. It doesn't matter how big or small the task is. When I was 18, my parents moved to Hershey, PA, and I worked yet another graveyard shift at the Hershey Reese's factory. They offered fifty cents more an hour for overnight work, and to me, that was a no-brainer. I could pull all-nighters at the factory and still lifeguard during the day that summer before I joined Army ROTC later that fall. It was hard, exhausting work, but it was also phenomenal. I was surrounded by people who were salt of the Earth folks and as good as the peanut butter cups we packed and the KitKats we baked. I was basically Willy Wonka that summer. Lesson #5 Stay Humble Eventually, I moved on to more buttoned-up work. When I earned my J.D., being proactive instead of reactive was something I learned early on in the law. In America, we don't really have one justice system--we have two. One for people who can afford lawyers, and another for those just trying to get by without one. That's why I was proud to serve on the board of the Legal Services Corporation, which funds more than 200 legal aid clinics across the country. But my start was humble. I didn't attend a prestigious law school that earns you a Supreme Court clerkship or plush job in Manhattan or DC. I attended Widener Commonwealth University School of Law in Harrisburg, Pennsylvania--a school most of you probably haven't heard of. It wasn't fancy, but I earned a great education. While there, I became the student clinic leader at the Harrisburg Civil Law Clinic under Professor Palmer Lockard. Our ragtag team of law students helped anyone who walked through the door: a single mom who needed a will, a retiree fighting off a fraudulent lawsuit, someone who just needed help filling out a job application. At the same time, I was president of the St. Thomas More Society, which volunteered at the . What struck me most was how often the people we served hot meals to were the very same people who needed legal help, but had no idea the law clinic even existed a few miles away. So we brought the law to them. Once a month, we set up a table in the soup kitchen and offered legal services right there. It was one of the most gratifying experiences of my career. To sit across from someone, look them in the eyes, and give them even the smallest measure of hope--hope that someone was willing to fight for them--was the kind of job that stays with you. Lesson #6 Bigger isn 't Always Better If you're someone who attends one of the elite Law Schools, first of all, congrats. Chances are, you are considering a career in Big Law, which is understandable since the starting salary for a first-year associate is over $200k in the major markets. But beware that Big Law is not nearly as glamorous as people think. There are more than 50,000 law firms in America, but the top 100--the so-called Am Law 100 --are treated like a league of their own. (They're called "white-shoe firms," a term so pretentious it needed its own vocabulary.) I know from experience. I've worked at two of them. After five years of active duty in the JAG Corps, where I tried cases before federal court and military martials, I knew I was ready for the big leagues. After months of interviews and being unemployed (having turned down the first job I was offered), I joined Cozen O'Connor, a first-generation mid-size Big Law firm co-founded by Patrick O'Connor, a fellow King's College graduate. The O'Connor family was practically its own legal dynasty. I had worked for Michael O'Connor as a messenger when I was 14, and for Tom O'Connor--who later became a judge--while I was at King's College. To finally work for Pat, one of the true legal titans of his generation, felt like a full-circle moment. The reality was less glamorous. There were nights I literally slept in the office and shaved in the bathroom with razors I picked up at the local 7-Eleven. It wasn't what I pictured when I imagined practicing at a major firm. But I was home, back in Philadelphia after years away, and working alongside some of the best lawyers in the country was truly an honor. I also met the mother of my children there. For a skinny kid from Northeast Philly who started full-time at community college, it was an opportunity I'll never forget, and one I'll always be grateful the O'Connor brothers gave me. Lesson #7 The Best Jobs Aren 't Jobs At All** The greatest job I'll ever have is being a dad. It's cliche, but true. If you read my piece , you know how emotional I was about my daughter Maggie starting college at Clemson and joining ROTC. That's how close we are. And I'm just as close to my 15-year-old son, Jack. Being a parent is a never-ending job that involves sacrifice. But it's always worth it. There were jobs and opportunities I turned down so I could coach Jack in hockey year after year. I'll never forget leaving the Pentagon late at night, driving straight through until morning just to make one of his tournaments in Canada. Maggie, meanwhile, was a competitive dancer for over a decade before switching to high school volleyball. I made it to almost every recital and every game. I don't regret a single missed professional opportunity I turned down for them. But the best part of all those years wasn't the tournaments or the recitals, it was the rides to and from practice. The rule was simple: whoever sat in the front seat couldn't be on their phone. We had to talk. Those conversations are what I'll treasure most. Letting them DJ the music, on the other hand, is one thing I don't. I know as those rides become few and far between, the bond will carry on for decades. Those were some of our most heartfelt conversations. I've had so many jobs over the years, it's hard to keep track. But what they all have in common is this: a job is never just a job. It's a story and a lesson. Sometimes it's humiliation, sometimes it's a small triumph. Sometimes a huge, legacy-defining triumph. But whatever it is, every job reveals something deeper about yourself that you carry with you into the next chapter of your life. So, as people go back to school or return to work from summer vacation, remember your job doesn't solely define you. But the sum of your experiences do shape who you are. Each experience deserves its own measure of pride. And yes, not to be overly sentimental, but being a dad is the greatest job I've ever had and the title I'll always be proudest to hold. -- Patrick The Secretary's SitRep is a reader-supported publication. name: Roy Cooper
subject: Yet again — NC is the most consequential swing state on the map
disclaimer_text: Paid for by State Democrats Committee (StateDemocrats
body:
Join me in the fight.
#
Peter — North Carolina is a swing state, and we can be sure that Republicans will go all out to defeat us. But I know how to win North Carolina. _Whether it was Attorney General or Governor, I’ve done it over and over in the most competitive years._
It takes the dedication to connect with folks in every community, the commitment to fight for the rights that people hold dear, and the courage to stand up in the face of threats to the progress we’ve made.
**I served as Governor for eight years. I know that North Carolinians don’t want to go backward. And based on the enthusiasm we’re seeing for our campaign from people across our country, that sentiment is true for Americans everywhere.**
As a U.S. Senator, I will fight for our rights to the ballot box, reproductive health care, clean air and drinking water, and more. I will work to make health care _more_ affordable and accessible — not yank it away from those most in need. I’ll advocate for better public schools, more clean energy options, and opportunities for good-paying jobs. I will defend our democracy.
And I’ll never stop listening to what _the people_ need from me and meeting them where they are.
I believe that this is how you win a swing state, Peter. And I _know_ that — with you on our team — we can succeed here in North Carolina.
**[I’m asking you to split a contribution between my campaign and State Democrats Committee because _right now_ is when we can build the momentum to win. Chip in $10 today.](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/94285?refcode=em20250903-9370&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1&akid=9370%2E614464%2EeQ0ttB)**
**[DONATE](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/94285?refcode=em20250903-9370&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=2&akid=9370%2E614464%2EeQ0ttB)**
Grateful to have you with me,
Roy Cooper
[](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/94285?refcode=em20250903-9370&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=3&akid=9370%2E614464%2EeQ0ttB)

name: Wes Moore
subject: Can you and I Facetime soon, Peter?
body:
I’d love to give you a call soon.
# [](https://act.wesmoore.com/go/8?t=1&akid=552%2E1086043%2ErHi92M)
Peter, if I called you over FaceTime, would you pick up? 📱

I’m planning to personally call some of our grassroots donors over the next few days, and I’d love to call you to say thank you for having my back and for supporting our fight for Maryland.

name: Gavin Newsom
subject: Mail-in voting
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Yes on 50, The Election Rigging Response Act, Governor Newsom’s Ballot Measure Committee
body:
Do not think for a second Donald Trump is not serious when he says he wants to bring an end to mail-in voting.

Folks,
Do not think for a second Donald Trump is not serious when he says he wants to bring an end to mail-in voting.
[](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/erra-em?refcode=em250903&refcode2=2514_4767699_dFHYYL&akid=2514%2E4767699%2EdFHYYL)
This is not a president who believes in free and fair elections. He tried to light democracy on fire after he lost in 2020. He dialed for 12,000 votes and now he's doing it in plain sight before the first vote is cast in 2026 and people are like "ahh, it's just Trump being Trump."
This is serious.
This is not going to be a normal election where both sides run ads, people vote, and the winner wins and it's just that simple.
He openly admits he wants to rig elections by killing mail-in voting!
He sent ICE agents to threaten and harass people outside our Election Rigging Response Act event.
So I am going to need all of you in this fight.
This is not just about redistricting or the lines we draw on maps -- it's about the line we have to hold to protect our democracy.
Thank you,
Gavin Newsom
* * *
i️ To my fellow Democrats — don't scroll past this. 🇺🇸
I have heard the calls do "Do something!" and I agree. If Texas wants to redraw their maps, California will fight fire with fire.
God help us if we're not successful. We may have enjoyed our last free and fair election. They're redrawing districts to hold the House; going after ActBlue and Democrats' ability to fundraise; going after colleges and the free press... We have to act.
[**Please make a contribution to help us get it done. The amount isn't as important as the donation itself. I need to know you're with me as we press forward.**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/erra-em?refcode=em250903&refcode2=2514_4767699_dFHYYL&akid=2514%2E4767699%2EdFHYYL)
_If you've stored your info with ActBlue, we'll process your contribution instantly:_
| | **[CONTRIBUTE $20](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/erra-em?express_lane=true&amount=20&refcode=em250903&refcode2=2514_4767699_dFHYYL&akid=2514%2E4767699%2EdFHYYL)**
---
| **[CONTRIBUTE OTHER](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/erra-em?express_lane=true&refcode=em250903&refcode2=2514_4767699_dFHYYL&akid=2514%2E4767699%2EdFHYYL)**
---

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com . Email is a very important way we communicate with supporters like you, but if you want to, you can [ unsubscribe here](https://act.gavinnewsom.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe?t=1&akid=2514%2E4767699%2EdFHYYL).
Paid for by Yes on 50, The Election Rigging Response Act, Governor Newsom’s Ballot Measure Committee. Committee Top Funders: HMP, California Teachers Association, Newsom for California – Governor 2022.

Grassroots donors like you pitched in to print them ... now, we’re buying stakes and other supplies we need to post them across VA-HD22. The 51-49 Democratic majority in the Virginia House of Delegates could be decided in this district THIS NOVEMBER ... expanding our majority will be our next chance to deliver Donald Trump and MAGA Republicans a huge defeat. Our MAGA Republican opponent is putting signs of his own up across the district ... we can’t keep up without you. If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately. ** Thanks for your support, -Team Guzman | Democrat is running for Delegate in Virginia's 22nd District.
