name: Derek Merrin
subject: 🚀 Let’s make September our strongest month yet
body:
With your help, we can hit our goal and keep building momentum.
| | |
---
| Peter,
September is here -- and this is our chance to show how strong our grassroots team is this year.
This month's fundraising goal is the most important benchmark of our campaign so far, as it is the final month before our first major fundraising deadline. If we hit it, we'll prove that our grassroots team has the energy, momentum, and support to flip Ohio's 9th and finish the mission we started together.
We came within less than one percent of winning in 2024. That means victory is within reach -- but only if we build early and strong.
👉 That's why I am asking, [can you chip in $10, $25, or even $50 today to help us hit our September goal?](https://a6x6czhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001HbSYJp7zgx_GnNYwZv-ZZ0GeQ8quufG9f2HdcP2gyZ6zbVhWEEAXFCjUgtW75eN08MqK9Ym0EZA_4iv9a90mKOHUwK73MANnBXBhlaqN4j-_kBbgZ-nPkWeq6uqy2TVq32gBabr_hmaxAsorDw7Bq2LEDxR_FsCSIkSZiUG4JuNsKMn0r_vmw5YAXodqbiIqJT7yav0PWWU=&c=-D1PDtkkGswgLyxGxPFlrS4JKeqNbPB8CvBAiSCaoOMl2hK9UQEBXw==&ch=3iuiT1XUjWShYbqeMAFQqegMe_ddB9Yjc9mvvN5ah-FbZe_b48JF7Q==)

---
| | [HELP ME HIT OUR GOAL NOW »](https://a6x6czhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001HbSYJp7zgx_GnNYwZv-ZZ0GeQ8quufG9f2HdcP2gyZ6zbVhWEEAXFCjUgtW75eN08MqK9Ym0EZA_4iv9a90mKOHUwK73MANnBXBhlaqN4j-_kBbgZ-nPkWeq6uqy2TVq32gBabr_hmaxAsorDw7Bq2LEDxR_FsCSIkSZiUG4JuNsKMn0r_vmw5YAXodqbiIq9cVrw-4iQvQ=&c=-D1PDtkkGswgLyxGxPFlrS4JKeqNbPB8CvBAiSCaoOMl2hK9UQEBXw==&ch=3iuiT1XUjWShYbqeMAFQqegMe_ddB9Yjc9mvvN5ah-FbZe_b48JF7Q==)
---
| Every grassroots donation today helps us:
✅ Get our message out across the district ✅ Build the ground game we need to win ✅ Push back against the Democrat attack machine
Together, we can make September our best month yet -- and with your help, we'll finish the mission in 2026.
---
| | [FINISH THE MISSION](https://a6x6czhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001HbSYJp7zgx_GnNYwZv-ZZ0GeQ8quufG9f2HdcP2gyZ6zbVhWEEAXFCjUgtW75eN08MqK9Ym0EZA_4iv9a90mKOHUwK73MANnBXBhlaqN4j-_kBbgZ-nPkWeq6uqy2TVq32gBabr_hmaxAsorDw7Bq2LEDxR_FsCSIkSZiUG4JuNsKMn0r_vmw5YAXodqbiIqjtdUPV6ph2A=&c=-D1PDtkkGswgLyxGxPFlrS4JKeqNbPB8CvBAiSCaoOMl2hK9UQEBXw==&ch=3iuiT1XUjWShYbqeMAFQqegMe_ddB9Yjc9mvvN5ah-FbZe_b48JF7Q==)

| [Let's finish what we started.](https://a6x6czhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001HbSYJp7zgx_GnNYwZv-ZZ0GeQ8quufG9f2HdcP2gyZ6zbVhWEEAXFCjUgtW75eN08MqK9Ym0EZA_4iv9a90mKOHUwK73MANnBXBhlaqN4j-_kBbgZ-nPkWeq6uqy2TVq32gBabr_hmaxAsorDw7Bq2LEDxR_FsCSIkSZiUG4JuNsKMn0r_vmw5YAXodqbiIqd3os4zlDUf4=&c=-D1PDtkkGswgLyxGxPFlrS4JKeqNbPB8CvBAiSCaoOMl2hK9UQEBXw==&ch=3iuiT1XUjWShYbqeMAFQqegMe_ddB9Yjc9mvvN5ah-FbZe_b48JF7Q==)

Derek Merrin Republican for Congress -- OH-09

name: Rebekah Jones
subject: Trickle-down democracy
body:

# [Trickle-down democracy](https://public-api.wordpress.com/bar/?stat=groovemails-events&bin=wpcom_email_click&redirect_to=http%3A%2F%2Fgeojones.org%2F2025%2F09%2F03%2Ftrickle-down-democracy%2F&sr=0&signature=64d8024db61507160a96fd805530c708&blog_id=26166017&user=248418217&_&_z=z)

[](https://geojones2.wordpress.com?action=user_content_redirect&uuid=e736967d7bf656c7fd3cb9d45b7e6891c8472b664f37c1e0bcbdc40348506c5f&blog_id=26166017&post_id=3658&user_id=248418217&subs_id=487468588&signature=c8242bb787cc4c4240bc587b2645816a&email_name=new-post&user_email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&encoded_url=aHR0cHM6Ly9zdWJzdGFja2Nkbi5jb20vaW1hZ2UvZmV0Y2gvJTI0c18hVTBRYSEsZl9hdXRvLHFfYXV0bzpnb29kLGZsX3Byb2dyZXNzaXZlOnN0ZWVwL2h0dHBzJTNBJTJGJTJGc3Vic3RhY2stcG9zdC1tZWRpYS5zMy5hbWF6b25hd3MuY29tJTJGcHVibGljJTJGaW1hZ2VzJTJGOWJiY2U5OGItZDdjNC00NTcwLTk4NmQtZTg2YTc1NGQzMTYyXzEwMjR4MTUzNi5wbmc&email_id=8c15a1c143e36de153e75080c94515d1)
The United States once celebrated its elections as a triumph of democracy, a ritual of participation and voice.
In recent years, though, the process has come to resemble theater, with corporate donors and foreign interests as directors and the public cast as unwilling extras. The script is written before voters even arrive, and when they question the futility of their role, they are chastised for refusing to perform.
**[Subscribe now](https://geojones2.wordpress.com?action=user_content_redirect&uuid=f1e73ed0d8386046b4949e86b83710534300ed4e8e67ab5a50be54948e7a4dba&blog_id=26166017&post_id=3658&user_id=248418217&subs_id=487468588&signature=3361586c2c36ebe25b6fb368e9cf8c55&email_name=new-post&user_email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&encoded_url=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cubWVzb3NjYWxlbmV3cy5jb20vc3Vic2NyaWJl&email_id=8c15a1c143e36de153e75080c94515d1)**
The 2024 election stripped the pretense away.
What should have been a decisive rejection of authoritarianism instead became an indictment of the Democratic Party itself. Rather than galvanize its base, Democrats alienated key communities, distancing themselves from trans allies, shelving popular social policies like universal pre-K and healthcare, and endorsing genocide abroad.
The consequences were catastrophic — not only for the country, but for a world still tethered to the promise of U.S. democracy.
The numbers told the story.
[Turnout ](https://geojones2.wordpress.com?action=user_content_redirect&uuid=89e14b44207230f6cde2d88a6dfb05c497922c793b5df35045880ad74fca6070&blog_id=26166017&post_id=3658&user_id=248418217&subs_id=487468588&signature=6069ad0dfe054c9913cb35ee70a4fe39&email_name=new-post&user_email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&encoded_url=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cucGV3cmVzZWFyY2gub3JnL3BvbGl0aWNzLzIwMjUvMDYvMjYvdm90ZXItdHVybm91dC0yMDIwLTIwMjQvP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9Y2hhdGdwdC5jb20&email_id=8c15a1c143e36de153e75080c94515d1)reached 63.7%, high by historical standards[ but down ](https://geojones2.wordpress.com?action=user_content_redirect&uuid=fca64ab9cbd0b1921603168880841186d1cf2e4192fd80ed578da9499ad49205&blog_id=26166017&post_id=3658&user_id=248418217&subs_id=487468588&signature=ab402f991e5b3f7fafd16ad01d6714a1&email_name=new-post&user_email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&encoded_url=aHR0cHM6Ly9iYWxsb3RwZWRpYS5vcmcvRWxlY3Rpb25fcmVzdWx0cyxfMjAyNDpfQW5hbHlzaXNfb2Zfdm90ZXJfdHVybm91dF9pbl90aGVfMjAyNF9nZW5lcmFsX2VsZWN0aW9u&email_id=8c15a1c143e36de153e75080c94515d1)from 66.6% in 2020.
Republicans gained an edge among infrequent voters, 54% of whom backed Trump compared to just 42% for his Harris. Trump expanded his coalition in ways once unimaginable: nearly doubling his support among Black voters, from 8% in 2020 to 15% in 2024, and winning 46% of Hispanic voters, a fourteen-point jump that flipped battleground states. Even young people, long considered Democrats’ strongest flank, showed cracks. While 47% turned out, many young men and suburban nonwhite voters shifted rightward or disengaged entirely.
None of this happened in a vacuum.
The Democratic Party leaned heavily on a fundraising model that rewards spectacle over solutions.
In the 2023–24 cycle, presidential candidates raised more than $2 billion, congressional candidates $3.8 billion, and party committees $2.7 billion. PACs alone accounted for nearly two-thirds of contributions, about $5.6 billion. “Dark money” soared to $1.9 billion, nearly doubling the previous cycle. Yet despite this torrent of cash, the Democratic National Committee limped into 2025 with just $15 million in reserves compared to the Republican National Committee’s $80 million. Money was everywhere, but none of it translated into genuine political vision.
That imbalance shaped not only outcomes but priorities.
For decades Democrats played chicken with abortion rights, treating Roe v. Wade as a perpetual campaign prop instead of codifying it into law. When Roe fell, they were left flat-footed, reduced to fundraising off the very crisis they had enabled.
Climate change was handled the same way. Poll after poll showed it ranked among voters’ top concerns — especially young people — but fossil fuel lobbying consistently outweighed voter demand. Climate only entered the agenda after disaster struck, wrapped in vague promises that never materialized.
The pattern was unmistakable. If trickle-down economics concentrates wealth at the top while leaving millions behind, **trickle-down democracy** concentrates power in the same way.
Decisions are made at the top, access hoarded by the wealthy and well-connected, and ordinary people told their role is simply to keep showing up and fall in line. The reward for loyalty is more empty gestures. The punishment for disengagement is blame.
**[Subscribe now](https://geojones2.wordpress.com?action=user_content_redirect&uuid=f1e73ed0d8386046b4949e86b83710534300ed4e8e67ab5a50be54948e7a4dba&blog_id=26166017&post_id=3658&user_id=248418217&subs_id=487468588&signature=3361586c2c36ebe25b6fb368e9cf8c55&email_name=new-post&user_email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&encoded_url=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cubWVzb3NjYWxlbmV3cy5jb20vc3Vic2NyaWJl&email_id=8c15a1c143e36de153e75080c94515d1)**
In this system, those with money, power, and access see their demands translated into legislation, while everyone else is told to fall in line even when the policies actively harm them because Democrats insist the alternative would be worse.
Instead of a bottom-up model, where people set the aims of their party, the party imposes its own agenda from the top down, narrowing the space for dissent and flattening the voices of its base. It is governance by ultimatum rather than consensus, and it is unsustainable. A democracy that conditions participation on obedience to elite priorities is no democracy at all — it is coercion disguised as choice.
It is no surprise, then, that trust has collapsed.
A quarter of Americans now describe themselves as politically homeless. Record numbers believe there is no meaningful difference between the parties. In interviews, voters in states like Pennsylvania described Democratic leaders as out of touch, lacking vision, and more concerned with polls than people.
This was not apathy — it was recognition. Recognition that the party claiming to defend democracy had reduced it to performance, to a campaign slogan, to the hollow act of being better than the alternative.
**[Subscribe now](https://geojones2.wordpress.com?action=user_content_redirect&uuid=f1e73ed0d8386046b4949e86b83710534300ed4e8e67ab5a50be54948e7a4dba&blog_id=26166017&post_id=3658&user_id=248418217&subs_id=487468588&signature=3361586c2c36ebe25b6fb368e9cf8c55&email_name=new-post&user_email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&encoded_url=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cubWVzb3NjYWxlbmV3cy5jb20vc3Vic2NyaWJl&email_id=8c15a1c143e36de153e75080c94515d1)**
The cracks became even clearer during the genocide in Gaza.
The Democratic Party’s foundational commitments to free speech, academic freedom, racial justice, and civil liberties eroded. Student protests and encampments that should have ignited solidarity instead prompted panic and repression.
At Columbia University, President Minouche Shafik authorized mass arrests during pro-Palestinian encampments-- the first such crackdown since 1968 — while suspending students and stoking fear. Across the country, institutions banned tents, restricted protest times, punished mask-wearing, and even handed faculty syllabi to congressional committees under pressure from political leaders.
> **_A stark contrast: BLM vs Gaza_**
> In 2020, the Democratic base rallied behind the Black Lives Matter movement — embracing it as both a moral imperative and political beacon. During that summer, a striking 45% of Congress began publicly posting “Black Lives Matter” on social platforms for the first time, a real-time testament to solidarity growing in the halls of power. Public rituals followed: Democratic National Convention (DNC) speakers held moments of silence for George Floyd and framed racial justice as central to national renewal. “When this moment ends,” urged Philonise Floyd, “let’s make sure we never stop saying their names.”
> Contrast this with today’s reaction to pro-Palestinian protests against genocide in Gaza — where the tone has shifted sharply. Rather than empathizing with protestors, many Democrats reflexively frame dissent as dangerous or destabilizing — or worse, tacit support for terrorism. Rather than amplifying the call for justice, the response has too often been silence or suppression. Party leaders blocked Palestinian voices at the 2024 DNC, and have echoed conservative talking points, citing “order” over moral urgency; the same protest tactics once treated as expression of civic conscience have now been redefined as threats to be neutralized.
Rather than defend students, Democrats largely collapsed into silence, or worse, joined calls for suppression.
Many echoed Republicans in criminalizing pro-Palestinian speech or punishing symbolic solidarity.
**[Subscribe now](https://geojones2.wordpress.com?action=user_content_redirect&uuid=f1e73ed0d8386046b4949e86b83710534300ed4e8e67ab5a50be54948e7a4dba&blog_id=26166017&post_id=3658&user_id=248418217&subs_id=487468588&signature=3361586c2c36ebe25b6fb368e9cf8c55&email_name=new-post&user_email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&encoded_url=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cubWVzb3NjYWxlbmV3cy5jb20vc3Vic2NyaWJl&email_id=8c15a1c143e36de153e75080c94515d1)**
When a Palestinian student at Columbia, Mahmoud Khalil, was arrested and threatened with deportation, Democratic voices hedged, calling only for “due process” instead of offering clear defense. By fall 2024, more than 100 universities had tightened speech restrictions, and the so-called “Palestine exception” to free expression had become a norm.
The betrayal did not end on campus. Suppression seeped into legislation and executive policy.
Trump’s executive order redefining antisemitism to chill pro-Palestinian advocacy forced universities to police speech or risk losing federal funds. Though his name was on the order, Democratic support (or acquiescence) ensured such frameworks stuck. A party once rooted in civil liberties now embraced surveillance and censorship, empowering police crackdowns, endorsing doxing campaigns, and mainstreaming the very tactics that Trump would later adopt and expand.
**[Subscribe now](https://geojones2.wordpress.com?action=user_content_redirect&uuid=f1e73ed0d8386046b4949e86b83710534300ed4e8e67ab5a50be54948e7a4dba&blog_id=26166017&post_id=3658&user_id=248418217&subs_id=487468588&signature=3361586c2c36ebe25b6fb368e9cf8c55&email_name=new-post&user_email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&encoded_url=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cubWVzb3NjYWxlbmV3cy5jb20vc3Vic2NyaWJl&email_id=8c15a1c143e36de153e75080c94515d1)**
By the time Trump returned to power, the handoff was seamless. The architecture of repression was already built. University presidents ousted, encampments raided, speech criminalized — these were not isolated incidents but test runs. They signaled that dissent could be policed, that constitutional rights could be suspended whenever politically expedient.
Trump simply took what Democrats had normalized and pushed it to the extreme: ordering federal crackdowns, expanding surveillance, and threatening mass expulsions. What Democrats began as a betrayal of principle became, in Trump’s hands, a blueprint for dismantling democracy altogether.
Both systems — economic and political — are sold with the same promise: that benefits delivered at the top would eventually reach everyone else. “Elect us and we’ll bring economic and political justice for all.”
**[Subscribe now](https://geojones2.wordpress.com?action=user_content_redirect&uuid=f1e73ed0d8386046b4949e86b83710534300ed4e8e67ab5a50be54948e7a4dba&blog_id=26166017&post_id=3658&user_id=248418217&subs_id=487468588&signature=3361586c2c36ebe25b6fb368e9cf8c55&email_name=new-post&user_email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&encoded_url=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cubWVzb3NjYWxlbmV3cy5jb20vc3Vic2NyaWJl&email_id=8c15a1c143e36de153e75080c94515d1)**
Trickle-down economics promised prosperity but delivered inequality, concentrating wealth in fewer hands while millions were left behind.
Trickle-down democracy functions the same way. Power and access is hoarded by the political class and its funders , while ordinary citizens are told their role is to legitimize a system designed to ignore them. Both create staggering inequities — one of wealth, the other of rights. Both leave those at the bottom waiting for relief that never comes. And both collapse under the weight of their own contradictions.
This is where America stands. A political order masquerading as democracy while hollowing out the freedoms it claims to protect. A party that once carried the banner of civil liberties now policing speech, sidelining social progress, and normalizing repression. A generation that called for justice and found their voices criminalized.
The lesson is not abstract. When freedom is rationed, when speech is conditional, when participation is demanded but not respected, democracy ceases to be democracy. It becomes spectacle. It becomes performance. And eventually, it becomes authoritarianism in everything but name. Democrats, by abandoning their principles, prepared the stage. Trump simply stepped into the role they had already written.
**[Subscribe now](https://geojones2.wordpress.com?action=user_content_redirect&uuid=f1e73ed0d8386046b4949e86b83710534300ed4e8e67ab5a50be54948e7a4dba&blog_id=26166017&post_id=3658&user_id=248418217&subs_id=487468588&signature=3361586c2c36ebe25b6fb368e9cf8c55&email_name=new-post&user_email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&encoded_url=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cubWVzb3NjYWxlbmV3cy5jb20vc3Vic2NyaWJl&email_id=8c15a1c143e36de153e75080c94515d1)**
The true danger of trickle-down democracy is not only that it fails. It is that it convinces people failure is the only option left.
A party that hoards power at the top while demanding loyalty from the bottom cannot expect infinite patience. People will disengage, not out of apathy but out of clarity, recognizing that their voices no longer matter. Unless Democrats abandon trickle-down democracy and embrace a true bottom-up model, they will not only forfeit elections, but the future of democracy itself.
Calling on democrats to do better, be better, and uphold the values that catalyzed a nation to elect Presidents Obama and Biden — anti-war, pro-science, pro-humanity — is not undermining efforts to fight back against fascism, as some centrist and right-leaning democrats might say. It’s a call for necessary reform to ensure we can win that fight.
Unless power flows up from the people, democracy will wither from the top down.

* * *
**trickle-down democracy** _noun_
1. A political system in which decision-making power, rights, and representation are concentrated among elites ,such as corporate donors, party leaders, and well-connected interests, while the broader public is expected to legitimize the system through participation without meaningful influence.
2. By analogy to _trickle-down economics_ , the theory that benefits extended to those at the top will eventually “trickle down” to everyone else, _trickle-down democracy_ describes the hollowing out of democratic practice: voters are promised representation but receive only symbolic gestures, delayed reforms, or conditional rights, while substantive power remains hoarded by the few.
3. A model of governance that produces widespread disillusionment, erodes trust in institutions, and creates conditions ripe for authoritarian exploitation.

_Example:_ “By treating elections as theater for corporate donors and suppressing dissent, the party has embraced trickle-down democracy—power at the top, crumbs for everyone else.”

clean_body: | You’re receiving this email because you have been an engaged reader of my newsletter, Mesoscale News. A recent error deleted most of my subscriber list, and I hope you’ll re-subscribe and help me to rebuild my network. Click here to su... | or | | | or ---|---|--- | | By Rebekah Jones on September 3, 2025 ---|--- You’re receiving this email because you have been an engaged reader of my newsletter,. A recent error deleted most of my subscriber list, and I hope you’ll re-subscribe and help me to rebuild my network. The United States once celebrated its elections as a triumph of democracy, a ritual of participation and voice. In recent years, though, the process has come to resemble theater, with corporate donors and foreign interests as directors and the public cast as unwilling extras. The script is written before voters even arrive, and when they question the futility of their role, they are chastised for refusing to perform. The 2024 election stripped the pretense away. What should have been a decisive rejection of authoritarianism instead became an indictment of the Democratic Party itself. Rather than galvanize its base, Democrats alienated key communities, distancing themselves from trans allies, shelving popular social policies like universal pre-K and healthcare, and endorsing genocide abroad. The consequences were catastrophic — not only for the country, but for a world still tethered to the promise of U.S. democracy. The numbers told the story. 63.7%, high by historical standards 66.6% in 2020. Republicans gained an edge among infrequent voters, 54% of whom backed Trump compared to just 42% for his Harris. Trump expanded his coalition in ways once unimaginable: nearly doubling his support among Black voters, from 8% in 2020 to 15% in 2024, and winning 46% of Hispanic voters, a fourteen-point jump that flipped battleground states. Even young people, long considered Democrats’ strongest flank, showed cracks. While 47% turned out, many young men and suburban nonwhite voters shifted rightward or disengaged entirely. None of this happened in a vacuum. The Democratic Party leaned heavily on a fundraising model that rewards spectacle over solutions. In the 2023–24 cycle, presidential candidates raised more than $2 billion, congressional candidates $3.8 billion, and party committees $2.7 billion. PACs alone accounted for nearly two-thirds of contributions, about $5.6 billion. “Dark money” soared to $1.9 billion, nearly doubling the previous cycle. Yet despite this torrent of cash, the Democratic National Committee limped into 2025 with just $15 million in reserves compared to the Republican National Committee’s $80 million. Money was everywhere, but none of it translated into genuine political vision. That imbalance shaped not only outcomes but priorities. For decades Democrats played chicken with abortion rights, treating Roe v. Wade as a perpetual campaign prop instead of codifying it into law. When Roe fell, they were left flat-footed, reduced to fundraising off the very crisis they had enabled. Climate change was handled the same way. Poll after poll showed it ranked among voters’ top concerns — especially young people — but fossil fuel lobbying consistently outweighed voter demand. Climate only entered the agenda after disaster struck, wrapped in vague promises that never materialized. The pattern was unmistakable. If trickle-down economics concentrates wealth at the top while leaving millions behind, trickle-down democracy concentrates power in the same way. Decisions are made at the top, access hoarded by the wealthy and well-connected, and ordinary people told their role is simply to keep showing up and fall in line. The reward for loyalty is more empty gestures. The punishment for disengagement is blame. In this system, those with money, power, and access see their demands translated into legislation, while everyone else is told to fall in line even when the policies actively harm them because Democrats insist the alternative would be worse. Instead of a bottom-up model, where people set the aims of their party, the party imposes its own agenda from the top down, narrowing the space for dissent and flattening the voices of its base. It is governance by ultimatum rather than consensus, and it is unsustainable. A democracy that conditions participation on obedience to elite priorities is no democracy at all — it is coercion disguised as choice. It is no surprise, then, that trust has collapsed. A quarter of Americans now describe themselves as politically homeless. Record numbers believe there is no meaningful difference between the parties. In interviews, voters in states like Pennsylvania described Democratic leaders as out of touch, lacking vision, and more concerned with polls than people. This was not apathy — it was recognition. Recognition that the party claiming to defend democracy had reduced it to performance, to a campaign slogan, to the hollow act of being better than the alternative. The cracks became even clearer during the genocide in Gaza. The Democratic Party’s foundational commitments to free speech, academic freedom, racial justice, and civil liberties eroded. Student protests and encampments that should have ignited solidarity instead prompted panic and repression. At Columbia University, President Minouche Shafik authorized mass arrests during pro-Palestinian encampments-- the first such crackdown since 1968 — while suspending students and stoking fear. Across the country, institutions banned tents, restricted protest times, punished mask-wearing, and even handed faculty syllabi to congressional committees under pressure from political leaders. A stark contrast: BLM vs Gaza In 2020, the Democratic base rallied behind the Black Lives Matter movement — embracing it as both a moral imperative and political beacon. During that summer, a striking 45% of Congress began publicly posting “Black Lives Matter” on social platforms for the first time, a real-time testament to solidarity growing in the halls of power. Public rituals followed: Democratic National Convention (DNC) speakers held moments of silence for George Floyd and framed racial justice as central to national renewal. “When this moment ends,” urged Philonise Floyd, “let’s make sure we never stop saying their names.” Contrast this with today’s reaction to pro-Palestinian protests against genocide in Gaza — where the tone has shifted sharply. Rather than empathizing with protestors, many Democrats reflexively frame dissent as dangerous or destabilizing — or worse, tacit support for terrorism. Rather than amplifying the call for justice, the response has too often been silence or suppression. Party leaders blocked Palestinian voices at the 2024 DNC, and have echoed conservative talking points, citing “order” over moral urgency; the same protest tactics once treated as expression of civic conscience have now been redefined as threats to be neutralized. Rather than defend students, Democrats largely collapsed into silence, or worse, joined calls for suppression. Many echoed Republicans in criminalizing pro-Palestinian speech or punishing symbolic solidarity. When a Palestinian student at Columbia, Mahmoud Khalil, was arrested and threatened with deportation, Democratic voices hedged, calling only for “due process” instead of offering clear defense. By fall 2024, more than 100 universities had tightened speech restrictions, and the so-called “Palestine exception” to free expression had become a norm. The betrayal did not end on campus. Suppression seeped into legislation and executive policy. Trump’s executive order redefining antisemitism to chill pro-Palestinian advocacy forced universities to police speech or risk losing federal funds. Though his name was on the order, Democratic support (or acquiescence) ensured such frameworks stuck. A party once rooted in civil liberties now embraced surveillance and censorship, empowering police crackdowns, endorsing doxing campaigns, and mainstreaming the very tactics that Trump would later adopt and expand. By the time Trump returned to power, the handoff was seamless. The architecture of repression was already built. University presidents ousted, encampments raided, speech criminalized — these were not isolated incidents but test runs. They signaled that dissent could be policed, that constitutional rights could be suspended whenever politically expedient. Trump simply took what Democrats had normalized and pushed it to the extreme: ordering federal crackdowns, expanding surveillance, and threatening mass expulsions. What Democrats began as a betrayal of principle became, in Trump’s hands, a blueprint for dismantling democracy altogether. Both systems — economic and political — are sold with the same promise: that benefits delivered at the top would eventually reach everyone else. “Elect us and we’ll bring economic and political justice for all.” Trickle-down economics promised prosperity but delivered inequality, concentrating wealth in fewer hands while millions were left behind. Trickle-down democracy functions the same way. Power and access is hoarded by the political class and its funders , while ordinary citizens are told their role is to legitimize a system designed to ignore them. Both create staggering inequities — one of wealth, the other of rights. Both leave those at the bottom waiting for relief that never comes. And both collapse under the weight of their own contradictions. This is where America stands. A political order masquerading as democracy while hollowing out the freedoms it claims to protect. A party that once carried the banner of civil liberties now policing speech, sidelining social progress, and normalizing repression. A generation that called for justice and found their voices criminalized. The lesson is not abstract. When freedom is rationed, when speech is conditional, when participation is demanded but not respected, democracy ceases to be democracy. It becomes spectacle. It becomes performance. And eventually, it becomes authoritarianism in everything but name. Democrats, by abandoning their principles, prepared the stage. Trump simply stepped into the role they had already written. The true danger of trickle-down democracy is not only that it fails. It is that it convinces people failure is the only option left. A party that hoards power at the top while demanding loyalty from the bottom cannot expect infinite patience. People will disengage, not out of apathy but out of clarity, recognizing that their voices no longer matter. Unless Democrats abandon trickle-down democracy and embrace a true bottom-up model, they will not only forfeit elections, but the future of democracy itself. Calling on democrats to do better, be better, and uphold the values that catalyzed a nation to elect Presidents Obama and Biden — anti-war, pro-science, pro-humanity — is not undermining efforts to fight back against fascism, as some centrist and right-leaning democrats might say. It’s a call for necessary reform to ensure we can win that fight. Unless power flows up from the people, democracy will wither from the top down. ) trickle-down democracy noun 1. A political system in which decision-making power, rights, and representation are concentrated among elites ,such as corporate donors, party leaders, and well-connected interests, while the broader public is expected to legitimize the system through participation without meaningful influence. 2. By analogy to trickle-down economics , the theory that benefits extended to those at the top will eventually “trickle down” to everyone else, trickle-down democracy describes the hollowing out of democratic practice: voters are promised representation but receive only symbolic gestures, delayed reforms, or conditional rights, while substantive power remains hoarded by the few. 3. A model of governance that produces widespread disillusionment, erodes trust in institutions, and creates conditions ripe for authoritarian exploitation. Example: “By treating elections as theater for corporate donors and suppressing dissent, the party has embraced trickle-down democracy—power at the top, crumbs for everyone else.” References/Reading Ballotpedia. name: Trump Alert via Team Griswold
subject: Trump is targeting Colorado directly
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jena for Colorado
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/2509-q3-jg [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/2509-q3-jg]
ICYMI: Trump targeted Colorado yesterday over mail ballots.
Yesterday, Trump claimed Colorado elections are “crooked” because of mail ballots. Remember, he voted a mail ballot in 2020 and encouraged Republican voters to use mail ballots just last year.
Trump says that mail ballots are one of the reasons that he has decided to move the US Space Command from Colorado Springs to Alabama. From a purple state that didn’t support him, to a red state that did.
Colorado voters rejected him three times in our free and fair elections, and he blames mail ballots instead of himself and his failing policies. We know there’s something “crooked” here...it's the president himself. Chip in any amount you can to elect an Attorney General who knows how to take him on, and win. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/2509-q3-jg]
It’s been proven time and again that voting by mail is safe and secure. As Secretary of State, Jena has taken on Trump, Mike Lindell, Tina Peters, and all of the far-right conspiracy theorists. As Attorney General, she will continue to protect the rights of Coloradans and stand up against threats to our democracy.
But she can’t win this race without your support. Rush in a donation today to support Jena’s campaign for AG and send the message that Coloradans won’t stand for Trump’s lies or intimidation. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/2509-q3-jg]
Thanks,
Team Griswold

name: NDRC
subject: Why we’ve got to stop the power grab in Missouri
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the National Democratic Redistricting Committee, John Bisognano, President
body:
Missouri Republicans face a choice: Listen to their constituents, or bend to Trump’s will.
| [](https://click.everyaction.com/k/114540171/567349189/-3547583?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxMy8xLzg5Mjk0IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjhlN2JlODBlLWRmODgtZjAxMS1iNDg0LTYwNDViZGViNzQxMyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=DsyuedYpeASubaYB8-sRU_zt_G3Me5Eozom8fYdRE3U=&emci=3621db25-4388-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&emdi=8e7be80e-df88-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&ceid=8431045)
---

Peter, Missouri Governor Mike Kehoe just called a special session to gerrymander the state’s congressional map. Like they did in Texas, Republicans are once again taking marching orders from Washington instead of listening to their own constituents. But just like in Texas, if Missouri Republicans choose to move forward with this egregious attempt to rig their maps, they will be met with fierce opposition and outcry. Missourians, from proud union members to business leaders, have spent the last month speaking out to oppose this mid-decade gerrymander. And yet Missouri Republicans are pressing ahead anyway under intense pressure from the Trump White House. **Now, Missouri Republicans face a choice: Listen to Missourians who oppose this mid-decade gerrymander, or fold to Donald Trump’s demands.** [**The NDRC is purpose-built for moments like this one. This Republican power grab is spreading into more states, and we need to step up our efforts to fight back wherever democracy is under attack. Fund our fight as we work to ensure the will of the people wins the day in this crucial moment for the future of democracy.**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/114540174/567349201/1110433579?refcode=don_ndrc_tier1_email_MissouriSpecialSession_email_20250903-NDRC-Tier1-MissouriSpecialSessionRR_&utm_source=email&utm_medium=email&utm_term=don&utm_content=20250903-NDRC-Tier1-MissouriSpecialSessionRR_&utm_campaign=MissouriSpecialSession&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxMy8xLzg5Mjk0IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjhlN2JlODBlLWRmODgtZjAxMS1iNDg0LTYwNDViZGViNzQxMyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=DsyuedYpeASubaYB8-sRU_zt_G3Me5Eozom8fYdRE3U=) | _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_

clean_body: Missouri Republicans face a choice: Listen to their constituents, or bend to Trump’s will. | Peter, Missouri Governor Mike Kehoe just called a special session to gerrymander the state’s congressional map. Like they did in Texas, Republicans are once again taking marching orders from Washington instead of listening to their own constituents. But just like in Texas, if Missouri Republicans choose to move forward with this egregious attempt to rig their maps, they will be met with fierce opposition and outcry. Missourians, from proud union members to business leaders, have spent the last month speaking out to oppose this mid-decade gerrymander. And yet Missouri Republicans are pressing ahead anyway under intense pressure from the Trump White House. Now, Missouri Republicans face a choice: Listen to Missourians who oppose this mid-decade gerrymander, or fold to Donald Trump’s demands. = | If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: = | = = | = = | = You are a critical part of this work, and we’re so grateful for your support. — Team NDRC = = Since 2017, the NDRC has executed a comprehensive redistricting strategy that shifts the redistricting power, creating fair districts where Democrats can compete. I'm Retired Brigadier General Shawn Harris -- and **I'm running against the most dangerous voice in Congress, Marjorie Taylor Greene.** When I was a kid in rural Georgia, summers meant gathering under the big elm tree at my neighbor's house. That's where the county delivered free lunches -- peanut butter sandwiches, fruit, milk -- so no kid went hungry. We laughed, we played, and we looked out for each other. Now Marjorie Taylor Greene wants to gut SNAP and slash the local programs that made moments like that possible. She's forgotten what it means to look out for your neighbors. I never will. I served this nation for 40 years. I'm running for Congress to protect the lifelines that help our kids, our families, and our communities thrive. But I can't do it alone. And that’s not a statement I make lightly.

The Trump administration is taking its marching orders from a fringe conspiracy theorist who’s associated with white supremacists and 9/11 denialism – and it’s directly undermining our career intelligence professionals.

If you’re as fired up as I am to hold Trump accountable and take back the Senate for Democrats – please join me with a contribution to my reelection campaign:

[_**https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mw-email-250903**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/114542182/567355979/1341216162?refcode=MW_ATA_EM_FR_Loomer2_250903_T1_1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODY1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxMjg4NDY0YS1lMzg4LWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=AHwnLovPZnVuxUjo0L33y4Re2p5-i2iQYl4GlhTvcyU=)

Hear me out, Derek:

Weeks ago, I arranged a meeting at the National Geospatial-Intelligence Agency’s headquarters in Springfield, Virginia. Engagements such as these are a core part of my responsibility to provide oversight and support to our intelligence community. **And they have never been questioned or politicized... until now.**

Earlier this week, conspiracy theorist Laura Loomer discovered the unpublicized visit and launched a campaign of baseless attacks against me. And then just last night, Trump’s henchmen canceled my visit.

**I can’t overstate how unprecedented and dangerous this is.** This administration is taking its marching orders from Laura Loomer – a wackjob with a long history of outlandish fringe views, including 9/11 denialism, anti-Muslim harassment campaigns, and associations with white supremacists.

Let me be clear: I will never be deterred from carrying out my constitutional responsibilities. The business of government and the security of our nation will not be dictated by extremist attention-seekers.

**But for goodness sake – I can’t be in this fight alone. And right now, the silence from my Republican colleagues is speaking volumes.** It doesn’t matter how dangerous, reckless, or irresponsible the Trump administration acts, too many of my Republican colleagues would rather fall in line than stand up for what is right.

I’ve never been more determined to hold this critical seat for Democrats and flip the balance of power in Congress.

[_**Join me with an emergency donation today to help us fight back, defeat Trump’s Republicans in the midterms, and flip the clean_body: | | | = I’ll be honest, Derek: I’m livid. And that’s not a statement I make lightly. The Trump administration is taking its marching orders from a fringe conspiracy theorist who’s associated with white supremacists and 9/11 denialism – and it’s directly undermining our career intelligence professionals. If you’re as fired up as I am to hold Trump accountable and take back the Senate for Democrats – please join me with a contribution to my reelection campaign: = Hear me out, Derek: Weeks ago, I arranged a meeting at the National Geospatial-Intelligence Agency’s headquarters in Springfield, Virginia. Engagements such as these are a core part of my responsibility to provide oversight and support to our intelligence community. And they have never been questioned or politicized... until now. Earlier this week, conspiracy theorist Laura Loomer discovered the unpublicized visit and launched a campaign of baseless attacks against me. And then just last night, Trump’s henchmen canceled my visit. I can’t overstate how unprecedented and dangerous this is. This administration is taking its marching orders from Laura Loomer – a wackjob with a long history of outlandish fringe views, including 9/11 denialism, anti-Muslim harassment campaigns, and associations with white supremacists. Let me be clear: I will never be deterred from carrying out my constitutional responsibilities. The business of government and the security of our nation will not be dictated by extremist attention-seekers. But for goodness sake – I can’t be in this fight alone. And right now, the silence from my Republican colleagues is speaking volumes. It doesn’t matter how dangerous, reckless, or irresponsible the Trump administration acts, too many of my Republican colleagues would rather fall in line than stand up for what is right. I’ve never been more determined to hold this critical seat for Democrats and flip the balance of power in Congress. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will be processed immediately: = ******=**** ****=**** ****=**** ****=**** ****=**** The fight continues, Mark Warner ******=****** | | Friends of Mark Warner 1490-5A Quarterpath Road #213 Williamsburg, VA 23185 United States | Click here to . | Paid for by Friends of Mark Warner

name: Maxine Dexter
subject: No one’s above the law
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Maxine for Congress
body:
https://maxinefororegon.com/ [https://maxinefororegon.com/]
Hey Peter,
Even those sworn to uphold the law must answer to it.
That’s why Rep. Jasmine Crockett and I introduced the CLEAR ID Act —to make sure immigration enforcement officers can’t hide behind masks or deceptive tactics.
Our tax dollars shouldn’t fund operations in which officers conceal their identities. If they’re entering our communities, they should do so face-to-face, openly, and transparently. They need to identify themselves verbally and visibly, so people understand what’s going on, so they can cooperate, so they can seek recourse if someone abuses their power.
Too often, we hear of people in street clothes, unmarked vehicles, and masks grabbing people off the street or at their school, hospital, or place of work and denying them their rights.
If our neighbors and loved ones have to show or prove their IDs, so should these officers. If our medical and tax records can be used to track Trump’s targets, those carrying out his orders need to identify themselves, too.
And now, criminals are impersonating these officers to rob and attack people.
The CLEAR ID Act protects everyone: it safeguards communities, strengthens public trust, and helps officers by making it harder for impostors to operate.
This is about accountability, transparency, and safety—plain and simple. If that’s how you think the United States of America should operate, rush $5 or $10 today to help us take back the House and demand accountability. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_med_email-25q3?refcode=x_250903_clearid_act&amount=10]
DONATE TODAY [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_med_email-25q3?refcode=x_250903_clearid_act]
Undaunted,
Maxine

Now she's using those skills in Congress to ensure healthy communities by standing up to Big Pharma, fighting climate change, and expanding reproductive rights, as well as rebuilding a fair economy for all by protecting Social Security and Medicare.
To learn more about Maxine, visit MaxineForOregon.com [https://maxinefororegon.com/] .

name: Jason Crow
subject: This campaign is about you
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jason Crow for Congress
body:
Jason Crow For Colorado [https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP21/1/88523/images/Screenshot%202025-08-21%20at%2010.50.41%E2%80%AFAM.png]
Happy September, team!

Let me take a moment and say thank you. This generous team keeps me fighting for what matters most in Congress.

This campaign is — and has always been — about you. It’s about tackling the issues that hold working families back. It’s about making sure everybody, no matter their zip code, can achieve their dreams. Your support and feedback help me do just that.

Now, I’m hoping to hear directly from you: Will you take a moment and share your feedback in my new September Priorities Survey today? [https://act.jasoncrowforcongress.com/a/092025-survey]

TAKE THE SURVEY [https://act.jasoncrowforcongress.com/a/092025-survey]

I made a pledge during my first victory speech: As long as I have the honor of serving you in Congress, I will be there for you no matter what .

As a former Army Ranger, I’ve worked to bring servant leadership to the halls of Congress. My team and I are working to put the needs of our community ahead of partisanship or political gridlock in Washington.

Share your thoughts on my new September Priorities Survey now. [http://act.jasoncrowforcongress.com/a/092025-survey]

TAKE THE SURVEY [https://act.jasoncrowforcongress.com/a/092025-survey]

Together, we will usher a new generation of leadership for the Democratic Party and Congress. Onward!

Jason

name: Nancy Mace for Governor
subject: Nancy Mace to toughen penalties for animal abusers
body:
Nancy Mace has been a strong and outspoken defender of South Carolina’s animals, from introducing legislation in Congress ... READ MORE | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/nancymace/nancy-mace-to-toughen-penalties-for-animal-abusers?e=ddc57b5ede)
---
| |
---
| | **NANCY MACE TO TOUGHEN PENALTIES FOR ANIMAL ABUSERS** Nancy Mace has been a strong and outspoken defender of South Carolina’s animals, from introducing legislation in Congress to standing up against outrageous cases of injustice here at home. Her message is clear: when it comes to protecting the voiceless, South Carolina must do better. “We’re fighting to protect ALL of God’s creatures,” **said Nancy Mace.** “When I’m Governor, criminals will be treated like criminals, and heroes will get the recognition they deserve.” Recently, Mace spoke out about a heartbreaking story in Greenville. Max, a dog left behind in 97° heat, suffered in silence. When Ashley and Beth from Anakin’s Trails Stray & Pet Recovery tried to save him, they were arrested. Meanwhile, the neglect and suffering went unpunished. “Justice was upside down in this case. A helpless animal was abandoned, yet the women who tried to help were the ones in handcuffs. That’s not justice, and it will not stand when I’m Governor,”**said Mace.**
---
|
---
| [Donate](https://nancymace.us14.list-manage.com/track/click?u=f826f615915b06bdee33acac8&id=dd676b2c69&e=ddc57b5ede "Donate")
---
| **Ending Animal Cruelty** In Congress, Nancy Mace has fought to protect animals and end abuse:
* **Violet’s Law (AFTER Act) –** Requires the retirement and rehoming of animals from federal research labs, giving them the chance at a real life.

* **Preventing Animal Abuse and Waste (PAAW) Act** – Bans painful taxpayer-funded experiments on dogs and cats and increases transparency in federal research.

* **TRANS MICE Act** – Ends taxpayer funding for unnecessary and unethical transgender-related animal experiments.

* **PRIMATES Act** – Seeks to halt invasive and cruel experiments on non-human primates funded by taxpayers.

* **PIGS Act** – Prohibits the use of gestation crates for pregnant pigs, improving standards in factory farming.

* **Mink Farming Ban** – Ends mink farming in the U.S. to protect public health and stop cruelty in the fur industry.

**As South Carolina’s next Governor, Nancy Mace will:**
* Create an Animal Abuse Registry so abusers can’t hide and repeat their crimes.
* Reform South Carolina’s animal cruelty laws to ensure they protect animals, not abusers.
* End the prosecution of good Samaritans who step in to save animals from dangerous and life-threatening conditions.
* Work with shelters, rescues, and communities to strengthen adoption and foster programs.

“If you’ve seen what we have done in Congress, imagine what we can do together for South Carolina’s animals,” **said Nancy Mace.** “We will clean up the broken system, protect the vulnerable, and make sure justice is served, for both people and pets.”
---
| [Donate](https://nancymace.us14.list-manage.com/track/click?u=f826f615915b06bdee33acac8&id=3ffcfb2036&e=ddc57b5ede "Donate")
---

campaign@nancymace.org
---
|
---

name: Roy Cooper
subject: Extreme Republicans want to take away your freedom
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Cooper for North Carolina
body:
Americans have fought for our freedom before. We can do it again. |
# [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/114542883/567357802/823540626?refcode=ea.5d26729d-e588-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&ms=ea.5d26729d-e588-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODE3NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1ZDI2NzI5ZC1lNTg4LWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=JjL71BlNEhr1DB8xmSOsSf6-CR9-Im6YQbZl74sVDPg=&emci=15afd819-d988-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&emdi=5d26729d-e588-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&ceid=17205535)
**I want you to know something about this team, Peter: If you believe some of the things that make America great are liberty, democracy, and acceptance, this campaign is for you.** Extreme Republicans plan to limit people’s rights so only a select few have the power to decide our nation’s future or the freedom to determine how to live. They want to roll back reproductive rights, voting rights, civil rights, LGBTQ+ rights, and more because they don’t believe that the lives of all Americans are equally important. And they’re willing to destroy our democracy itself to achieve their aim. **Peter, I still believe that government by and for the people is worth fighting for — and that means fighting for liberty and justice for all Americans.** It means speaking out for people who need a voice. It means defending the people that our federal government has made a mission of attacking. It means securing access to health care, including abortion care. It means reaffirming our commitment to our democratic system of government — a system that is under threat like never before. What’s happening in Washington and from extreme Republicans nationwide is just wrong, Peter. It’s up to us to stop it. **[Give whatever you can if you are with me in the fight.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/114542884/567357804/-1743631236?refcode=ea.5d26729d-e588-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&ms=ea.5d26729d-e588-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODE3NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1ZDI2NzI5ZC1lNTg4LWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=JjL71BlNEhr1DB8xmSOsSf6-CR9-Im6YQbZl74sVDPg=)** | **[DONATE](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/114542886/567357805/-1743631236?refcode=ea.5d26729d-e588-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&ms=ea.5d26729d-e588-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODE3NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1ZDI2NzI5ZC1lNTg4LWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=JjL71BlNEhr1DB8xmSOsSf6-CR9-Im6YQbZl74sVDPg=)**

clean_body: Americans have fought for our freedom before. We can do it again. | I want you to know something about this team, Peter: If you believe some of the things that make America great are liberty, democracy, and acceptance, this campaign is for you. Extreme Republicans plan to limit people’s rights so only a select few have the power to decide our nation’s future or the freedom to determine how to live. They want to roll back reproductive rights, voting rights, civil rights, LGBTQ+ rights, and more because they don’t believe that the lives of all Americans are equally important. And they’re willing to destroy our democracy itself to achieve their aim. Peter, I still believe that government by and for the people is worth fighting for — and that means fighting for liberty and justice for all Americans. It means speaking out for people who need a voice. It means defending the people that our federal government has made a mission of attacking. It means securing access to health care, including abortion care. It means reaffirming our commitment to our democratic system of government — a system that is under threat like never before. What’s happening in Washington and from extreme Republicans nationwide is just wrong, Peter. It’s up to us to stop it. = | = Thank you, Roy Cooper = Democrat Roy Cooper is running for U.S. Senate to help take back the Majority for Democrats. body:
We’re facing a challenge our team never saw coming

[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/114543025/567358249/-240735101?refcode=email-header&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NTc1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwOGMyNTYwOS1lNjg4LWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=hOxk0nPu31YeOHqpPv6zXEsMD0oC3utOz4XZapP7rlQ=) Hi — I hope you took some time to rest and relax over your Labor Day weekend. I wanted to quickly reach out and say thank you for helping us reach our August fundraising goal. I know my team and I sent you a lot of emails over the last few days, so thank you for bearing with us. But just as one challenge ends, another begins. And we’re facing a challenge our team never saw coming.**For all intents and purposes, our Election Day has moved forward a full calendar year.** Here’s why: Recently, Republicans all across Kansas announced their support to follow in Texas’ footsteps, re-draw our state’s Congressional maps without giving voters a say, and kick me out of office. At this very moment, extremists are pushing this Trump-backed plan to the Kansas Legislature. **That means what we do in the next few months, or even the next few days, could be the difference between holding this must-win seat — or losing it to National Republicans for a generation.** I can’t tell you exactly how the upcoming weeks will unfold. But I do know that this team has won tough fights before — and with your help, we can do it again. [_**Despite what Republicans may believe, this district doesn’t belong to a political party. And it doesn’t belong to me either. It belongs to _you_ — and no politician should be able to take that power away from you. If you’re with me, and you’re ready to defend KS-03 from Trump’s gerrymandering, I could really use your help at this unprecedented moment for our campaign.**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/114543026/567358250/-2097022923?refcode=email_090325_belong&amounts=5%2C25%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NTc1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwOGMyNTYwOS1lNjg4LWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=hOxk0nPu31YeOHqpPv6zXEsMD0oC3utOz4XZapP7rlQ=) | | [Donate →](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/114543027/567358251/-2097022923?refcode=email_090325_belong&amounts=5%2C25%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NTc1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwOGMyNTYwOS1lNjg4LWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=hOxk0nPu31YeOHqpPv6zXEsMD0oC3utOz4XZapP7rlQ=)

Fellow Conservative,

Congress is like a battlefield—thrilling, mission-driven, and full of conflict. At least no one's shooting at us here. Well, not with guns, anyway.

The out-of-touch fake news "reporters" at Politico decided they would target the most dangerous men in DC: **Navy SEALs.**

We all ran for office because we were still hungry to protect and fight for our country. The war may be over for us, but we can't abandon the part of our lives that made us who we are. We were part of an elite unit on the front lines, and Congress is somewhere else we can be out front doing something special for our country.

_**Yet the liberal news corporations, who _desperately_ want to keep the status quo, _attacked us_ for wanting to take on the Swamp establishment and business-as-usual going on in our government.**_

At least no one's shooting at us here. Well, not with guns, anyway. The out-of-touch fake news "reporters" at Politico decided they would target the most dangerous men in DC: Navy SEALs. We all ran for office because we were still hungry to protect and fight for our country. The war may be over for us, but we can't abandon the part of our lives that made us who we are. name: IlhanOmar.com
subject: Chicago
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY ILHAN FOR CONGRESS
body:
Yesterday, Trump made it clear that we are days away from federal troops and other federal law enforcement being sent into Chicago for an all-out takeover.
# [](https://act.ilhanomar.com/go/35?t=1&akid=1793%2E791427%2E_YlUBc)

[](https://act.ilhanomar.com/signup/em-sep25-military/?source=em250903-1-90&akid=1793%2E791427%2E_YlUBc)
Friend — Yesterday, Trump made it clear that we are days from federal troops and other federal law enforcement being sent into Chicago for an all-out takeover.
Illinois Governor JB Pritzker announced that federal agents as well as Texas National Guardsmen are being positioned near Chicago in anticipation of a probable military takeover.
Just like in DC, the Trump administration is planning to use local crime as an excuse to send ICE into Latino neighborhoods and carry out mass deportations. Just like in DC, we will see people’s civil rights violated, activists targeted, families separated, and entire communities intimidated into silence.
**[Trump is acting like a military dictator. Ilhan Omar is pushing Congress to act. Please, add your name to our petition today and show your opposition to Trump’s authoritarian tactics.**](https://act.ilhanomar.com/signup/em-sep25-military/?source=em250903-1-90&akid=1793%2E791427%2E_YlUBc)
**[ADD YOUR NAME](https://act.ilhanomar.com/signup/em-sep25-military/?source=em250903-1-90&akid=1793%2E791427%2E_YlUBc)**
Again, friend, this is not about crime.
Crime has been falling in most major US cities, including Chicago. And Chicago’s local leadership has said for weeks that they don’t want or need a federal takeover. They need more investment in their communities to end the cycles that create crime. They need common sense gun laws nationwide to bring down gun violence.
But Trump needs an excuse to unleash an authoritarian military takeover, and crime is all too easy of an issue to use as a scare tactic on Fox News.
So let’s set the record straight. Trump does not have the constitutional authority to trample on state and local laws and take over America’s cities unless there is a real emergency.
He may think he has all of the power, but Congress has the ability and the responsibility to put a check on this out of control administration.
That’s why we’re asking: **[Sign on to Ilhan Omar’s petition today calling on Congress to take action to protect American cities from indefinite military occupation.**](https://act.ilhanomar.com/signup/em-sep25-military/?source=em250903-1-90&akid=1793%2E791427%2E_YlUBc)
**[SIGN ON NOW](https://act.ilhanomar.com/signup/em-sep25-military/?source=em250903-1-90&akid=1793%2E791427%2E_YlUBc)**
Military takeovers that lead to the constant surveillance and harassment of regular people in the streets are not a solution to crime, immigration, or any other issue that we need to tackle.
It’s simply unAmerican, and we must fight it every step of the way — not capitulate to a far-right, authoritarian vision of life
In solidarity,
Team Ilhan Omar

PAID FOR BY ILHAN FOR CONGRESS
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a very important way we communicate with supporters like you, but if you want to, you can **[unsubscribe here](https://act.ilhanomar.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?source=em-footer&t=2&akid=1793%2E791427%2E_YlUBc)**. You can also **[sign up to receive fewer emails](https://act.ilhanomar.com/signup/fewer-emails/?source=em-footer&t=3&akid=1793%2E791427%2E_YlUBc)**.
Submit this form to [**update your contact information**](https://act.ilhanomar.com/signup/update-contact-info/source=em-footer?t=4&akid=1793%2E791427%2E_YlUBc)
Ilhan is counting on our people-powered grassroots movement to fund this campaign. If you can, stand with us by **[making a contribution today](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/io-em-footer/?refcode=em-footer&t=5&refcode2=1793_791427__YlUBc&akid=1793%2E791427%2E_YlUBc)**.
Ilhan for Congress
PO Box 33079
Washington D.C., 20033

name: Jenna Burns
subject: My police officer husband is being prosecuted by Fani Willis
body:
| | [View in Web Browser](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.com/click/e9728531/VaHR0cHM6Ly93cm8ubnVjbGV1c2VtYWlsLmNvbS9hbXBsaWZ5L3YvRWs2OUpta01iVA/SZW1haWxfYW1lcmljYU9ORXN0d2Fycm9vbV9jb20yNTA5MDM/LbmNsN3laUzY0UA/qP2hpZHM9bmN6MUJ0V3p2czV5dlBscw/gaLh0Ow/scvc85822a4)
|
---
Dear Patriot, If the name Fani Willis sounds familiar to you, there's a reason. She's the radical District Attorney in Georgia who's been abusing her office to target innocent police officers. And now, my husband is facing Fani Willis as she’s trying to lock him up for the _rest of his life_ for doing his job as a police officer and trying to keep our city safe. **[Before I ask for your urgent help, let me start at the beginning...](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.) ** My husband, James, served in the Atlanta Police Department. That's me, James, and our son in the photo I sent.
---
[](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.)
---
Being a police officer meant the world to James. He was born and raised in Atlanta, and saw his job as a way to help his neighbors. But if Fani Willis has her way in court... **life in prison** could be his reward. Our nightmare started nine years ago, when my husband responded to a call about a man breaking into a parked car at an apartment complex. When he got there, he saw a running car parked facing the wrong way on a one-way street. With his emergency lights on, James got out to investigate. _That's when it happened._ The suspect saw my husband, slammed on the gas, and drove straight at him. With only a split second to act, James drew his weapon and fired a single shot at the vehicle bearing down on him. It struck the suspect, who died instantly. James was shaken by the incident, but thankfully he was unhurt. He went home, certain he had done everything by the book. **So you can imagine our shock when James found out he was being charged with murder!** You see, James is white, and the suspect he shot was black. So the woke, anti-police prosecutors in Atlanta have made it their mission to destroy my husband. Now you understand why I'm so upset and why [**I'm turning to you for help.**](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.) James is an honest, hardworking, good-hearted man who only wants to help people. My husband is _exactly_ the kind of man you want keeping your city safe. But none of that matters to radical prosecutors like Fani Willis. She _knows_ what happened that night. She _knows_ James was in a life-or-death situation. She _knows_ that the charge should have been dropped years ago. Fani Willis just wants to make an example out of my husband. Patriot, for years we've been trapped in limbo. We've lost nearly all our friends, and we've even been forced to move because of the terrible lies the prosecutors have been spreading about my husband. By some miracle, we've been able to get by, but every day is difficult. As you can imagine after 9 years of prosecution, what's been truly overwhelming is the cost of James' legal defense. When we ran out of money, we had all but given up hope... **And then we got a call from Jason Johnson, the President of the Law Enforcement Legal Defense Fund, or LELDF.** If you've ever heard of LELDF, you know they're a national group fighting on behalf of wrongfully-accused police officers like James. Not only did the LELDF board decide to help my husband get the legal defense he deserves, they're also raising money to pay for the best attorneys so James can finally clear his name. And now, after his trial judge REFUSED to address an important motion and follow the law – James is heading to the Georgia Supreme Court. Patriot, that's why I'm writing to you today to ask you one of the toughest questions I've ever asked anyone... [**Could you send an emergency gift today to help defend James while he fights for justice?**](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.) His legal team is working day and night as they fight to get this motion addressed and get my husband declared NOT GUILTY once and for all. We have nowhere else to turn. And I'm so scared for what will happen to my husband as his trial can’t start until the Supreme Court addresses the issue. **[So, if you're able, can you send your emergency tax-deductible donation of $35, $50, $100, or even $500 or $1,000 to LELDF today to defend James before it’s too late?](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.) ** It's your [**emergency $35 gift**](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.) that could make the difference between defeating radical Fani Willis and ensuring justice and my husband spending the rest of his life in prison. Thank you for taking the time to read about my husband. And thank you in advance for helping my James.
---
Sincerely,
---
Jenna Burns
---
P.S. My husband did EVERYTHING he needed to survive that night. He doesn't deserve to die in prison. But that's EXACTLY what will happen if radical prosecutor Fani Willis gets her way. Please help James before it's too late! [**Please follow this link right now to make your emergency tax-deductible gift of $35 or more to LELDF.**](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.) Thank you for helping me save my husband and my family.
---
|
---
_More such officers who sacrifice so much for will face similar fates unless we speak out and stand up for the brave men and women in blue. The next time you see a police officer in your community, please thank them for their service. To read more about our mission or the other Law Enforcement officers we're defending, please visit. And remember –Blue Lives Matter!_ © Law Enforcement Legal Defense Fund | [www.PoliceDefense.org](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.)
2560 Huntington Ave. Suite 203, Alexandria, VA, 22303

---
---
---

108 S Washington St. Suite 200
Alexandria, VA 22314

And now, my husband is facing Fani Willis as she’s trying to lock him up for the rest of his life for doing his job as a police officer and trying to keep our city safe. My husband, James, served in the Atlanta Police Department. That's me, James, and our son in the photo I sent. Being a police officer meant the world to James. He was born and raised in Atlanta, and saw his job as a way to help his neighbors. But if Fani Willis has her way in court... life in prison could be his reward. Our nightmare started nine years ago, when my husband responded to a call about a man breaking into a parked car at an apartment complex. When he got there, he saw a running car parked facing the wrong way on a one-way street. With his emergency lights on, James got out to investigate. That's when it happened. The suspect saw my husband, slammed on the gas, and drove straight at him. With only a split second to act, James drew his weapon and fired a single shot at the vehicle bearing down on him. It struck the suspect, who died instantly. James was shaken by the incident, but thankfully he was unhurt. He went home, certain he had done everything by the book. So you can imagine our shock when James found out he was being charged with murder! You see, James is white, and the suspect he shot was black. So the woke, anti-police prosecutors in Atlanta have made it their mission to destroy my husband. Now you understand why I'm so upset and why James is an honest, hardworking, good-hearted man who only wants to help people. My husband is exactly the kind of man you want keeping your city safe. But none of that matters to radical prosecutors like Fani Willis. She knows what happened that night. She knows James was in a life-or-death situation. She knows that the charge should have been dropped years ago. Fani Willis just wants to make an example out of my husband. Patriot, for years we've been trapped in limbo. We've lost nearly all our friends, and we've even been forced to move because of the terrible lies the prosecutors have been spreading about my husband. By some miracle, we've been able to get by, but every day is difficult. As you can imagine after 9 years of prosecution, what's been truly overwhelming is the cost of James' legal defense. When we ran out of money, we had all but given up hope... And then we got a call from Jason Johnson, the President of the Law Enforcement Legal Defense Fund, or LELDF. If you've ever heard of LELDF, you know they're a national group fighting on behalf of wrongfully-accused police officers like James. Not only did the LELDF board decide to help my husband get the legal defense he deserves, they're also raising money to pay for the best attorneys so James can finally clear his name. And now, after his trial judge REFUSED to address an important motion and follow the law – James is heading to the Georgia Supreme Court. Patriot, that's why I'm writing to you today to ask you one of the toughest questions I've ever asked anyone... His legal team is working day and night as they fight to get this motion addressed and get my husband declared NOT GUILTY once and for all. We have nowhere else to turn. And I'm so scared for what will happen to my husband as his trial can’t start until the Supreme Court addresses the issue. It's your that could make the difference between defeating radical Fani Willis and ensuring justice and my husband spending the rest of his life in prison. Thank you for taking the time to read about my husband. And thank you in advance for helping my James. Sincerely, Jenna Burns P.S. My husband did EVERYTHING he needed to survive that night. He doesn't deserve to die in prison. But that's EXACTLY what will happen if radical prosecutor Fani Willis gets her way. Please help James before it's too late! Thank you for helping me save my husband and my family. | More such officers who sacrifice so much for will face similar fates unless we speak out and stand up for the brave men and women in blue. The next time you see a police officer in your community, please thank them for their service. To read more about our mission or the other Law Enforcement officers we're defending, please visit. And remember –Blue Lives Matter! © Law Enforcement Legal Defense Fund | 2560 Huntington Ave. Suite 203, Alexandria, VA, 22303

* * *
# Trump tears Communist China for anti-American conspiracy
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/2159660a-2174-40f2-8165-a7a5620ff74d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
President Donald Trump **[accused Chinese President Xi Jinping of conspiring](https://substack.com/redirect/2159660a-2174-40f2-8165-a7a5620ff74d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** with Russian President Vladimir Putin and North Korean leader Kim Jong Un "against The United States of America" as they attended a massive military parade in Beijing's Tiananmen Square marking 80 years since Japan's defeat in World War II.
### [Trump wrote:](https://substack.com/redirect/2159660a-2174-40f2-8165-a7a5620ff74d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
_" The big question to be answered is whether or not President Xi of China will mention the massive amount of support and "blood" that The United States of America gave to China in order to help it to secure its FREEDOM from a very unfriendly foreign invader. Many Americans died in China's quest for Victory and Glory. I hope that they are rightfully Honored and Remembered for their Bravery and Sacrifice! May President Xi and the wonderful people of China have a great and lasting day of celebration. Please give my warmest regards to Vladimir Putin, and Kim Jong Un, as you conspire against The United States of America."_
* **WWII Historical Reference:** Trump questioned whether Xi would acknowledge America's role in China's WWII victory, writing that the U.S. gave "massive amount of support and 'blood'" to help China secure freedom from "a very unfriendly foreign invader," hoping fallen Americans would be "rightfully Honored and Remembered."

* **Military Parade Context:** The event featured the three leaders walking together down a red carpet before taking their seats for what was described as China's largest-ever military parade, expected to include aerial displays, marching troops, and a procession of missiles and tanks with two dozen largely non-Western leaders attending.
* **Ongoing Diplomatic Tension:** The comments come as Trump's administration continues trade negotiations with China, having extended a tariff truce for 90 days to prevent duties from escalating dramatically, while also attempting to negotiate an end to Russia's war against Ukraine with Putin.

* * *
* * *
**[Together with Mode Mobile](https://substack.com/redirect/569cd21f-8225-495e-877c-489c737fa605?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**
# **[Investors are watching this fast-growing tech company](https://substack.com/redirect/569cd21f-8225-495e-877c-489c737fa605?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/569cd21f-8225-495e-877c-489c737fa605?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
No, it's not Nvidia... It's **Mode Mobile** , 2023's fastest-growing software company according to Deloitte.
Their EarnPhone has helped users earn and save over $325M, driving $75M+ in revenue and **[an eye-popping 32,481% growth](https://substack.com/redirect/569cd21f-8225-495e-877c-489c737fa605?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).** And having secured partnerships with **Walmart** and **Best Buy** , Mode's not stopping there...
Like Uber turned vehicles into income-generating assets, Mode is turning smartphones into **[an easy passive income source](https://substack.com/redirect/569cd21f-8225-495e-877c-489c737fa605?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). **The difference is that you still have a chance to invest in Mode's pre-IPO offering before the window closes.
They've just been granted the stock ticker _**$MODE**_ by the Nasdaq and over 50,000 investors participated in their previously sold out offering at $0.30/share.
**⏳[$52M+ already raised -- invest before the opportunity closes.](https://substack.com/redirect/569cd21f-8225-495e-877c-489c737fa605?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**
[Learn More](https://substack.com/redirect/569cd21f-8225-495e-877c-489c737fa605?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* * *
* * *
# Google soars $21 a share as antitrust case goes almost entirely in their favor
In a **[landmark antitrust ruling,](https://substack.com/redirect/82dd533a-53c7-4ca6-85fd-9c4ccb06775e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** a U.S. judge spared Google from selling Chrome or Android but ordered the tech giant to share search data with rivals, reshaping competition in Silicon Valley.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/82dd533a-53c7-4ca6-85fd-9c4ccb06775e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
* **Court Rejects Drastic Measures:** Judge Amit Mehta deemed selling Chrome or Android excessive, preserving Google's core assets while mandating data sharing to boost competition.
* **Apple Benefits Financially:** The ruling allows Google to maintain lucrative default search deals, funneling billions annually to Apple, strengthening its financial position.
* **Privacy Concerns Raised:** Google expressed worries about user privacy due to mandatory data sharing, as rivals gain access to sensitive search and advertising data.
* **AI Startups Gain Edge:** Shared data could empower AI-driven search competitors like ChatGPT, potentially challenging Google's dominance as the industry evolves rapidly.

* * *
* * *
# Treasury Dept. announces list of eligible jobs for "no tax on tips"
The Treasury Department **[released the official list of jobs](https://substack.com/redirect/e0277052-bbe1-4c7c-ae61-5cf38c765a61?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** covered by Trump's "no tax on tips" policy under the One Big Beautiful Bill Act, which provides a deduction of up to $25,000 for qualified tips received by tipped workers from 2025 through 2028.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/e0277052-bbe1-4c7c-ae61-5cf38c765a61?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
* **Beverage & Food Service:** Traditional restaurant workers including bartenders, wait staff, dishwashers, food servers, chefs, cooks, bakers, food preparation workers, and host staff at restaurants, lounges, and coffee shops are eligible for the deduction.
* **Entertainment & Hospitality:** The policy covers entertainers like dancers, musicians, comedians, DJs, digital content creators, casino dealers, hotel staff including bellhops, concierges, desk clerks, and housekeeping cleaners.
* **Personal & Home Services:** A broad range of service workers qualify, including personal care aides, event planners, photographers, pet caretakers, tutors, nannies, home repair workers, electricians, plumbers, and HVAC technicians.
* **Transportation & Recreation:** The deduction extends to taxi and rideshare drivers, valet attendants, delivery workers, golf caddies, sports instructors, tour guides, massage therapists, barbers, hairstylists, and fitness trainers, covering more than 60 tipped professions across eight industry categories.

---
